```{r Concentration equivalent plastique}

#Rentrer ici le ratio plastique:solvant en mg et mL (Exemple : 1g de plastique : 3 mL de DMSO --> 3300 mg : 1 ml)
plastique <- c(330)
solvant <- c(1)

```

La valeur de moyenne NaCl appelé "Mean_NaCl" est une moyenne des valeurs de bioluminescence pour chaque Temps de lecture. 

# MICROTOX PASAIA

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er set microtox Pasaia}

#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Pasaia")), c(111)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech1", "Ech2", "NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 2), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), 111) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, 18), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, 18), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 8), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech1 <- c(80, 82, 87, 90, 72, 75, 81, 84, 69, 69, 73, 75,    #Ech 1 replicat 1
          65, 85, 89, 88, 58, 80, 82, 83, 55, 73, 74, 74,   #Ech 1 replicat 2
          67, 86, 89, 91, 61, 80, 83, 85, 58, 74, 75, 76)   #Ech 1 replicat 3

Ech2 <- c(60, 70, 81, 85, 53, 64, 74, 79, 51, 63, 70, 72,    #Ech 2 replicat 1
          64, 74, 83, 88, 57, 68, 77, 80, 55, 65, 71, 74,   #Ech 2 replicat 2
          58, 73, 74, NA, 52, 66, 69, NA, 50, 64, 63, NA)    #Ech 2 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(93, 91, 91, NA, 84, 83, 83, NA, 75, 74, 74, NA)
DMSO <- c(94, 94, 91, 93, 88, 88, 84, 86, 83, 81, 75, 77)
K2CR2O7 <- c(37, 51, 64, 70, 86, 21, 41, 56, 64, 77, 10, 26, 42, 53, 63)

Biolu <- c(Ech1, Ech2, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch
     

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

I_Ech1 <- c(round((100-((Ech1 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech1[I_Ech1<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech2 <- c(round((100-((Ech2 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech2[I_Ech2<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch

 #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech1, I_Ech2, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Pasaia1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Pasaia1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```


```{r Informatisation des données du 2eme set microtox Pasaia}

#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Pasaia")), c(96)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech1", "Ech2", "NaCl", "DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 12, 12))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12)  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(24))        #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 2), Repli_control)    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(2)), 96) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, 18), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025, 0.0125)                                     #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, 18), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 8)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech1 <- c(77, 92, 86, 84, 75, 91, 84, 81, 74, 87, 77, 74,  #Ech 1 replicat 1
          78, 79, 88, 88, 76, 78, 86, 87, 74, 74, 81, 80,  #Ech 1 replicat 2
          72, 77, 82, 67, 69, 72, 80, 60, 67, 68, 75, 54)  #Ech 1 replicat 3

Ech2 <- c(60, 73, 79, 88, 57, 70, 78, 88, 56, 69, 74, 81,  #Ech 2 replicat 1
          61, 51, 81, 87, 59, 46, 80, 86, 59, 42, 77, 80,  #Ech 2 replicat 2
          58, 72, 83, NA, 55, 71, 81, NA, 55, 71, 79, NA)  #Ech 2 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(NA, 92, 91, NA, NA, 89, 88, NA, NA, 81, 81, NA)  #first control NaCl error = NA
DMSO <- c(95, 96, 86, NA, 94, 95, 80, NA, 89, 88, 73, NA)  
#K2CR2O7 <- c()                                            

Biolu <- c(Ech1, Ech2, NaCl, DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

   #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3], na.rm = T), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7], na.rm = T), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11], , na.rm = T), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch

I_Ech1 <- c(round((100-((Ech1 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))  #Do not touch
I_Ech1[I_Ech1<0] <- 0                                      #Do not touch

I_Ech2 <- c(round((100-((Ech2 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))  #Do not touch
I_Ech2[I_Ech2<0] <- 0                                      #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                      #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                       #Do not touch

      #---------------------------#

#I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2)) #Do not touch

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech1, I_Ech2, I_NaCl, I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Pasaia2 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Pasaia2                                                                                   #Change the name of the table                                                                        

```

```{r Informatisation des données DMSO du 3eme set microtox Pasaia}
#Add DMSO control set
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Pasaia")), c(12)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(12))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)),  c(12))  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(12))        #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 1))    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(3)), 12) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch                                  
Conc <- c(rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 1)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...
NaCl <- c(94, 92, 93, NA, 92, 92, 91, NA, 89, 88, 87, NA)
DMSO <- c(93, NA, 100, 97, 98, NA, 101, 100, 98, NA, 99, 95)

Biolu <- c(DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

  #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3], na.rm = T), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7], na.rm = T), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11], , na.rm = T), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch
      
I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                      #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                       #Do not touch

    #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Pasaia3 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Pasaia3                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_Pasaia0 <- rbind(df_Pasaia1, df_Pasaia2, df_Pasaia3)  #Do not touch
df_Pasaia0                                                              #Do not touch

mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Pasaia0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

#Calcul Biolu_pondéré avec le DMSO ramené à 100
df_Pasaia <- df_Pasaia0  %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% mutate(Biolu_p = round(((Biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100), digits = 3))
df_Pasaia

df_Pasaia$Ech <- as.character(df_Pasaia$Ech)

df_Pasaia <- df_Pasaia %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ),
    Inhi_p = 100-Biolu_p)
df_Pasaia 
```

```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Pasaia0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_Pasaia %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi = 100-round(mean(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi_sd = round(sd(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))#,
                  #"per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2),
                  #"per_Inhi_sd" = round((100-((sd_biolu / mean_DMSO$sd_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech

Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_Pasaia0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA)

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_DMSO

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_Pasaia0 %>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, per_Inhi_sd)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
df_Nacl_K2

Average_df_Pasaia <- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_Pasaia

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Pasaia1 <- Average_df_Pasaia %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO")
Average_df_Pasaia1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Pasaia2 <- Average_df_Pasaia %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_Pasaia2

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_Pasaia3 <- Average_df_Pasaia %>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_Pasaia3
```

## STATISTIC PASAIA

Comparison between : PA 0.35 new / PA 0.35 used / DMSO at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Comparision of variance: 
H0: The fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of Pasaia}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30
Stat_pasaia <-  df_Pasaia %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30")
Stat_pasaia

#H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_pasaia, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_pasaia$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_pasaia$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_pasaia$Biolu) # P-value a few lower 0.05 --> Proxi normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_pasaia) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_pasaia)
anova(Aov) # <0.05 --> On rejète Ho

#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified
```

Conclusion ANOVA:

P < 0.05 H0 rejected, the data support the Ha, the fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fishery


PostHoc Hypothesis: 
New vs used
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

New vs Crtl
H0: The new fishing net does not reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

Used vs Crtl
H0: The used fishing net does not reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The used fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_pasaia$Biolu, Stat_pasaia$Ech, method="bh")

TukeyHSD(Aov)

```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New - Used

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Dilu av. Tps}

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = Dilu_f4) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(min(5), max(Average_df_Pasaia2$per_Inhi, na.rm = T), by = 1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(name = "Temps d'exposition (minutes)", values = Tps_color) +
  labs(title = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence en fonction des concentrations \nde l'elutriat",
       subtitle ="New gillnet in PA from Pasaia (∅0.35mm)",  
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Volume (mL) d'élutriat de plastique (DMSO) dans 1 mL de milieu", 
       color = "Temps d'exposition (minutes)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0))) 
  
G1
```

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min}
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Inhibition as a function of exposure concentration \nto leachate", 
       subtitle = "Solvent: Dymethylsulfoxyde (DMSO) ratio 1g:3ml \nExposition time 30 minutes",
       y = "Average bioluminescence inhibition (%)", 
       x = "Samples", 
       fill = "Concentration") +

  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 40, by = 5)) +

        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom") 
  
G9
```

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Tps à Concentration max}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

G10 <- df_Pasaia %>% filter(Dilu == 1, grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Tps))) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5) + 
  labs(title = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence en fonction des échantillons \nà la concentration maximum", 
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Echantillons") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Temps d'exposition (minutes)", values = Tps_color) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 40, by = 2)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)))
G10

#_______________________________________________

G13 <- df_Pasaia %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% ggplot(aes(as.factor(Conc), Inhi_p, fill = as.factor(Ech))) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5) + 
  labs(title = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence en fonction des échantillons \nà la concentration maximum", 
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Echantillons")  +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 70, by = 2)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)))
G13
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

G12 <- Average_df_Pasaia2 %>% filter(Tps == 30 & Dilu == 1) %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=mean_biolu)) + 
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge(), fill=Color_biolu_Pasaia, width = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean_biolu-sd_biolu,ymax=mean_biolu+sd_biolu), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) + 
  labs(title = "Average bioluminescence for leachates of the different PA α Ø.35 gillnet", 
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic Plastic equivalent concentration in solvent: 3.3 mg/mL
",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = -2),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")) +
  annotate(geom="text", x= "DMSO", y=105, label="a",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5) +
  annotate(geom="text", x= "Ech1", y=79, label="b",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5)+
  annotate(geom="text", x= "Ech2", y=62, label="c",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5)
G12

G12 + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 202, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G12 <- Average_df_Pasaia2 %>% filter(Tps == 30 & Dilu == 1) %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=mean_biolu)) + 
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge(), fill=Color_biolu_Pasaia, width = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean_biolu-sd_biolu,ymax=mean_biolu+sd_biolu), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) + 
  labs(y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = -2),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")) +
  annotate(geom="text", x= "DMSO", y=105, label="a",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5) +
  annotate(geom="text", x= "Ech1", y=79, label="b",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5)+
  annotate(geom="text", x= "Ech2", y=62, label="c",
           color="black", parse = F, size = 4.5)
G12

G12 + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 194, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```

# MICROTOX ST-JEAN-DE-LUZ

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er set microtox St_Jean}

#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("St_Jean")), c(147)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech3", "Ech4", "Ech5", "NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 3), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), 147) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, 27), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, 27), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 11), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech3 <- c(88, 88, NA, 95, 90, 89, NA, 97, 90, 86, NA, 94,    #Ech 3 replicat 1
          84, 92, 94, 90, 86, 93, 94, 91, 86, 90, 91, 87,   #Ech 3 replicat 2
          86, 90, 94, 98, 88, 91, 96, 98, 89, 89, 93, 97)   #Ech 3 replicat 3

Ech4 <- c(86, 95, 95, 101, 89, 97, 96, 102, 87, 95, 93, 101,    #Ech 4 replicat 1
          88, 87, 86, 86, 93, 89, 93, 90, 92, 88, 89, 85,   #Ech 4 replicat 2
          81, 85, 89, 86, 84, 87, 94, 88, 81, 85, 90, 85)    #Ech 4 replicat 3

Ech5 <- c(85, 91, 94, 91, 88, 95, 96, 93, 88, 94, 92, 89,    #Ech 5 replicat 1
          87, 89, 93, 87, 97, 95, 96, 93, 97, 94, 92, 87,   #Ech 5 replicat 2
          79, 87, 88, NA, 84, 91, 93, NA, 82, 89, 91, NA)    #Ech 5 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(94, 92, 93, NA, 92, 92, 91, NA, 89, 88, 87, NA)
DMSO <- c(89, 92, 86, 89, 92, 93, 92, 94, 94, 93, 89, 91)
K2CR2O7 <- c(39, 53, 63, 72, 81, 25, 42, 51, 60, 74, 13, 32, 43, 62, 72)

Biolu <- c(Ech3, Ech4, Ech5, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

#Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech3 <- c(round((100-((Ech3 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech3[I_Ech3<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech4 <- c(round((100-((Ech4 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech4[I_Ech4<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech5 <- c(round((100-((Ech5 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech5[I_Ech5<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 
      
      #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech3, I_Ech4, I_Ech5, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_St_Jean1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_St_Jean1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Informatisation des données du 2eme set microtox St_Jean}
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("St_Jean")), c(132)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech3", "Ech4", "Ech5", "NaCl", "DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 12, 12))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12)  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(24))        #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 3), Repli_control)    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(2)), 132) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, 27), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025, 0.0125)                                     #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, 27), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 11)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech3 <- c(82, 95, 95, 96, 85, 98, 97, 98, 83, 96, 96, 94,    #Ech 3 replicat 1
          85, 96, 97, 98, 85, 98, 99, 99, 85, 97, 95, 96,   #Ech 3 replicat 2
          88, 94, 93, NA, 93, 97, 93, NA, 93, 95, 89, NA)   #Ech 3 replicat 3

Ech4 <- c(89, 106, 97, 97, 104, 118, 108, 106, 107, 122, 112, 111,    #Ech 4 replicat 1
          83, 102, 86, 92, 94, 116, 97, 104, 102, 121, 102, 106,   #Ech 4 replicat 2
          84, 84, 82, 79, 92, 93, 87, 84, 100, 96, 93, 88)    #Ech 4 replicat 3

Ech5 <- c(90, 96, 89, 95, 103, 109, 97, 107, 108, 116, 101, 107,    #Ech 5 replicat 1
          93, 84, 83, 92, 111, 96, 96, 106, 121, 101, 101, 110,   #Ech 5 replicat 2
          84, 89, 94, NA, 97, 98, 102, NA, 101, 103, 107, NA)    #Ech 5 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(94, 92, 93, NA, 92, 92, 91, NA, 89, 88, 87, NA)  #first control NaCl error = NA
DMSO <- c(94, 97, 97, 101, 107, 110, 109, 113, 118, 118, 115, 119)  
#K2CR2O7 <- c()                                            

Biolu <- c(Ech3, Ech4, Ech5, NaCl, DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

#Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech3 <- c(round((100-((Ech3 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech3[I_Ech3<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech4 <- c(round((100-((Ech4 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech4[I_Ech4<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech5 <- c(round((100-((Ech5 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech5[I_Ech5<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))  
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 #Do not touch
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 
      
      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech3, I_Ech4, I_Ech5, I_NaCl, I_DMSO) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_St_Jean2 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_St_Jean2                                                                                   #Change the name of the table
```

```{r Informatisation des données DMSO du 3eme set microtox St_Jean}
#Add DMSO control set
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("St_Jean")), c(12)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(12))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)),  c(12))  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), c(12))        #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 1))    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(3)), 12) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch                                  
Conc <- c(rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 1)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...
NaCl <- c(94, 92, 93, NA, 92, 92, 91, NA, 89, 88, 87, NA)
DMSO <- c(93, NA, 100, 97, 98, NA, 101, 100, 98, NA, 99, 95)

Biolu <- c(DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

#Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch
      
I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                      #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                       #Do not touch

      
      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch
#Creation du tableau initial
df_St_Jean3 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_St_Jean3                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_St_Jean <- rbind(df_St_Jean1, df_St_Jean2, df_St_Jean3)  #Do not touch
df_St_Jean                                                              #Do not touch

mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO


#Calcul Biolu_pondéré avec le DMSO ramené à 100
df <- df_St_Jean  %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% mutate(Biolu_p = round(((Biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100), digits = 3))
df
```

```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

Average_Ech <-  tibble(df %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi = 100-round(mean(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi_sd = round(sd(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))#,
                  #"per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2),
                  #"per_Inhi_sd" = round((100-((sd_biolu / mean_DMSO$sd_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech

Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA)

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_DMSO

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, per_Inhi_sd)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
df_Nacl_K2

Average_df_St_Jean <- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_St_Jean

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_St_Jean1 <- Average_df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO")
Average_df_St_Jean1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_St_Jean2 <- Average_df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_St_Jean2

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_St_Jean3 <- Average_df_St_Jean %>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_St_Jean3
```

## STATISTIC ST-JEAN-DE-LUZ

Comparison between : PE 4.25 new / PE 4.25 used / PE 4.25 used+ / DMSO at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Comparision of variance: 
H0: The fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of St_Jean}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30
Stat_St_Jean <-  df_St_Jean %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30")
Stat_St_Jean

#H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_St_Jean, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_St_Jean$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_St_Jean$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_St_Jean$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> Normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

#bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_St_Jean) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_St_Jean)
anova(Aov) # >0.05 --> On rejète Ho

#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified
```

Conclusion ANOVA:

P > 0.05 we cannot reject H0, the fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 5min}

                  Ech4 = "New gillnet panel \nn=6",
                  Ech5 = "Used gillnet panel \nn=6")

G9 <- Average_df_St_Jean1 %>% filter(Tps == 30) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Conc))) + 
  geom_col(position = 'dodge', width = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin= ifelse(per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd < 0, 0, per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd), ymax=per_Inhi+per_Inhi_sd), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Inhibition as a function of exposure concentration \nto leachate from St-Jean-de-Luz trawl net (Polyethylene ∅4.25 mm)", 
       subtitle = "Solvent: Dymethylsulfoxyde (DMSO) ratio 1g:3ml \nExposition time 30 minutes",
       y = "Average bioluminescence inhibition (%)", 
       x = "Samples", 
       fill = "Concentration") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Plastic eq. concentration in solvent (mg/ml)", values = Conc_color, guide = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 40, by = 2)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom") 
  
G9
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max}

Color_biolu_St <- c("Ech3" = "aquamarine", "Ech4" = "#36A7A7" , "Ech5" = "#054959", "DMSO" = "#A48A7B")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

G12 <- Average_df_St_Jean2 %>% filter(Tps == 30 & Dilu == 0.5) %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=mean_biolu)) + 
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge(), fill=Color_biolu_St, width = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean_biolu-sd_biolu, ymax=mean_biolu+sd_biolu), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) + 
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the leachate of PE ε Ø4.25 n", 
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 120, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))
G12

G12 + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p > 0.05") + 
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 194, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G12 <- Average_df_St_Jean2 %>% filter(Tps == 30 & Dilu == 0.5) %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=mean_biolu)) + 
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge(), fill=Color_biolu_St, width = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean_biolu-sd_biolu, ymax=mean_biolu+sd_biolu), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) + 
  labs(y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 120, by = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))
G12

G12 + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p > 0.05") + 
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))


```


# MICROTOX ARCACHON

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er set microtox Arca}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(183) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(4)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Arca")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech6", "Ech7", "Ech8", "Ech9", "NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb-15)/(Ech_nb*3)), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech6 <- c(51, 81, 84, 89, 45, 70, 72, 79, 41, 63, 65, 69,    #Ech 6 replicat 1
          76, 88, NA, 85, 67, 78, NA, 74, 61, 71, NA, 67,   #Ech 6 replicat 2
          72, 82, 80, 87, 62, 71, 70, 76, 56, 65, 62, 68)   #Ech 6 replicat 3

Ech7 <- c(81, 91, 91, NA, 72, 80, 79, NA, 64, 67, 66, NA,    #Ech 7 replicat 1
          84, 90, 90, NA, 74, 78, 77, NA, 67, 69, 65, NA,   #Ech 7 replicat 2
          87, 92, 92, NA, 76, 83, 81, NA, 70, 74, 70, NA)    #Ech 7 replicat 3

Ech8 <- c(67, 77, 82, 85, 55, 66, 71, 73, 51, 60, 63, 66,    #Ech 8 replicat 1
          43, 75, 73, 84, 37, 64, 65, 73, 31, 58, 58, 67,   #Ech 8 replicat 2
          69, 86, 86, NA, 59, 76, 75, NA, 54, 70, 69, NA)    #Ech 8 replicat 3

Ech9 <- c(79, 86, 85, NA, 80, 88, 83, NA, 74, 80, 78, NA,    #Ech 9 replicat 1
          76, 84, 80, NA, 81, 86, 85, NA, 75, 81, 78, NA,   #Ech 9 replicat 2
          83, 85, 84, NA, 82, 82, 83, NA, 75, 77, 79, NA)    #Ech 9 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(92, 86, 91, NA, 79, 74, 79, NA, 71, 67, 71, NA)
DMSO <- c(80, 82, 90, NA, 69, 71, 80, NA, 62, 65, 72, NA)
K2CR2O7 <- c(35, 43, 53, 68, 80, 22, 34, 46, 61, 71, 10, 23, 38, 54, 59)

Biolu <- c(Ech6, Ech7, Ech8, Ech9, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech6 <- c(round((100-((Ech6 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech6[I_Ech6<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech7 <- c(round((100-((Ech7 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech7[I_Ech7<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech8 <- c(round((100-((Ech8 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech8[I_Ech8<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech9 <- c(round((100-((Ech9 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech9[I_Ech9<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 

      #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech6, I_Ech7, I_Ech8, I_Ech9, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Arca1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Arca1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```


```{r Informatisation des données du 2eme set microtox Arca}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(168) #change the total row number in function of your samples

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech6", "Ech7", "Ech8", "Ech9", "NaCl", "DMSO")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(24))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)

Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb)/(Ech_nb*3))) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech6 <- c(55, 92, 84, 86, 51, 86, 79, 79, 49, 81, 74, 74,    #Ech 6 replicat 1
          49, 85, 87, 92, 51, 82, 82, 87, 48, 77, 78, 82,   #Ech 6 replicat 2
          74, 83, 82, 92, 68, 76, 76, 87, 64, 73, 72, 82)   #Ech 6 replicat 3

Ech7 <- c(82, 88, 94, NA, 83, 86, 91, NA, 79, 81, 85, NA,    #Ech 7 replicat 1
          83, 88, 86, NA, 83, 88, 86, NA, 80, 81, 79, NA,   #Ech 7 replicat 2
          80, 86, 85, NA, 80, 84, 84, NA, 77, 80, 76, NA)    #Ech 7 replicat 3

Ech8 <- c(77, 78, 84, 82, 67, 72, 78, 76, 64, 68, 74, 73,    #Ech 8 replicat 1
          72, 79, 80, 85, 63, 71, 73, 80, 63, 69, 70, 77,   #Ech 8 replicat 2
          73, 89, 84, NA, 66, 83, 79, NA, 65, 81, 77, NA)    #Ech 8 replicat 3

Ech9 <- c(82, 88, 93, NA, 85, 95, 97, NA, 84, 91, 94, NA,    #Ech 9 replicat 1
          86, 87, 91, NA, 92, 91, 93, NA, 90, 89, 91, NA,   #Ech 9 replicat 2
          81, 83, 91, NA, 87, 86, 95, NA, 86, 83, 90, NA)    #Ech 9 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(91, 92, 91, NA, 83, 86, 83, NA, 78, 80, 78, NA)
DMSO <- c(79, 86, 88, 88, 73, 80, 83, 84, 70, 77, 80, 79)

Biolu <- c(Ech6, Ech7, Ech8, Ech9, NaCl, DMSO) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech6 <- c(round((100-((Ech6 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech6[I_Ech6<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech7 <- c(round((100-((Ech7 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech7[I_Ech7<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech8 <- c(round((100-((Ech8 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech8[I_Ech8<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech9 <- c(round((100-((Ech9 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech9[I_Ech9<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 

      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech6, I_Ech7, I_Ech8, I_Ech9, I_NaCl, I_DMSO) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Arca2 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
```

```{r Informatisation des données DMSO du 3eme set microtox Arca}
#Add DMSO control set
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Arca")), c(48)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(48))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)),  c(48))  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_bio <- c(rep(NA, 48))    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(3)), 48) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch                                  
Conc <- c(rep(C_DMSO, 48)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 4)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...


DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(92, 99, 81)),4), rep(mean(c(80, 84, 71)), 4), rep(mean(c(71, 74, 63)), 4),
          rep(mean(c(81)),4), rep(mean(c(79)), 4), rep(mean(c(74)), 4),
          rep(mean(c(85, 94, 96)),4), rep(mean(c(90, 99, 101)), 4), rep(mean(c(88, 96, 100)), 4),
          rep(mean(c(88, 87)),4), rep(mean(c(93, 91)), 4), rep(mean(c(88, 87)), 4))

Biolu <- c(DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                     #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 48)                                       #Do not touch


      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Arca3 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Arca3                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_Arca0 <- rbind(df_Arca1, df_Arca2, df_Arca3)  #Do not touch
df_Arca0                                         #Do not touch

mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Arca0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

#Calcul Biolu_pondéré avec le DMSO ramené à 100
df_Arca <- df_Arca0  %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% mutate(Biolu_p = round(((Biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100), digits = 3))
df_Arca

df_Arca$Ech <- as.character(df_Arca$Ech)

df_Arca <- df_Arca %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "6") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.95 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "7") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.95 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "8") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "9") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm"
    ))
df_Arca 
```

```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Arca0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_Arca %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi = 100-round(mean(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi_sd = round(sd(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))#,
                  #"per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2),
                  #"per_Inhi_sd" = round((100-((sd_biolu / mean_DMSO$sd_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech

Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_Arca0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA)

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_DMSO

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_Arca0 %>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, per_Inhi_sd)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
df_Nacl_K2

Average_df_Arca <- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_Arca

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Arca1 <- Average_df_Arca %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "6") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.95 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "7") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.95 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "8") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "9") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm"
    ))
Average_df_Arca1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Arca2 <- Average_df_Arca %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_Arca2

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_Arca3 <- Average_df_Arca %>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_Arca3
```


## STATISTIC ARCACHON

Comparison between : PA 0.95 new / PA 0.95 used / PA 0.33 new / PA 0.33 used / DMSO at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Comparision of variance: 
H0: The fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of Arca}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30
Stat_Arca <-  df_Arca0 %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30")
Stat_Arca

#H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_Arca, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_Arca$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_Arca$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_Arca$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

#bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_Arca) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_Arca)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho

#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified
```

Conclusion ANOVA:

P < 0.05 H0 rejected, the data support the Ha, the fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


PostHoc Hypothesis: 

0)
New 0.95 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

1)
Used 0.95 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

8)
New 0.95 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

9)
Used 0.95 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

2)
New 0.95 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.95 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.95 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

3)
Used 0.95 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

4)
New 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.95 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.95 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

5)
Used 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

6)
New 0.95 vs used 0.95
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

7)
New 0.33 vs Crtl 0.33
H0: The new fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery



```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_Arca$Biolu, Stat_Arca$Ech, method="bh")

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

0) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.95 - New 0.33

1) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.95 - Used 0.33

8) p < 0.05 H0 rejected  
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.95 - Used 0.33

9) p < 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.95 - New 0.33

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.95

3) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.95

4) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.33

5) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.33

6) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.95 - Used 0.95

7) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.33 - Used 0.33
