```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 5min}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

Ech_Arca <- c(Ech6 = "New gillnet panel \nn=6", 
                  Ech7 = "Used gillnet panel \nn=6",
                  Ech8 = "New gillnet panel \nn=6",
                  Ech9 = "Used gillnet panel \nn=6")

G9 <- Average_df_Arca1 %>% filter(Tps == 30) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Conc))) + 
  geom_col(position = 'dodge', width = 0.5) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin= ifelse(per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd < 0, 0, per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd), ymax=per_Inhi+per_Inhi_sd), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) +
  facet_grid(~ factor(Diam), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.45, 0.45)) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Inhibition as a function of exposure concentration \nto leachate from Arcachon gillnet (Polyamide)",
       subtitle = "Solvent: Dymethylsulfoxyde (DMSO) ratio 1g:3ml \nExposition time 30 minutes",
       y = "Average bioluminescence inhibition (%)", 
       x = "Samples", 
       fill = "Concentration") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Plastic eq. concentration in solvent (mg/ml)", values = Conc_color, guide = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 60, by = 5)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Ech_Arca) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
G9
```

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Tps à Concentration max}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

G10 <- df_Arca %>% filter(Dilu == 1, grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Tps))) + geom_boxplot(width = 0.5) + 
  labs(title = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence en fonction des échantillons \nà la concentration maximum", 
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Echantillons") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Temps d'exposition (minutes)", values = Tps_color) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Echantillons) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 40, by = 2)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)))
G10
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

Color_biolu_Arca <- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech9" = "#054959",  "Ech7" = "#054959", "Ech8" = "aquamarine" ,  "Ech6" = "aquamarine")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

# compact letter display
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_Arca)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho

# compact letter display
tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey
# compact letter display
cld_Arca <- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_Arca
#Create column FN
FN_Arca <- c( "Control", "PA β Ø.95 n", "PA β Ø.95 u ", "PA β Ø.33 n", "PA β Ø.33 u")

FN_Arca
# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_Arca <- dt_Arca %>% group_by(Stat_Arca, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_Arca) %>%
  arrange(desc(w))
dt_Arca

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
cld_Arca <- as.data.frame.list(cld_Arca$Ech)
dt_Arca$cld_Arca <- cld_Arca$Letters
dt_Arca

#X axis
xaxis_Arca <- c(Ech9 = "PA β Ø.33 u \n(2400h - 3000h) \nn=6",
                DMSO = "Control \nn=6",
                Ech7 = "PA β Ø.95 u \n(2400h - 3000h) \nn=6",
                Ech8 = "PA β Ø.33 n \nn=6",
                Ech6 = "PA β Ø.95 n \nn=6")

G <- dt_Arca %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_Arca) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Arca, labels = xaxis_Arca) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the leachate of PA β Ø.95 & PA β Ø.33",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent (DMSO) 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_Arca, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G <- dt_Arca %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_Arca) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Arca, labels = xaxis_Arca) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "\n \n",
    y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_Arca, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 24) = 11.21, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```


# MICROTOX EPMO

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er set microtox EPMO}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(183) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(4)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("EPMO")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech10", "Ech11", "Ech12", "Ech13", "NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb-15)/(Ech_nb*3)), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech10 <- c(85, 89, 87, 89, 70, 70, 71, 71, 69, 68, 69, 70,    #Ech 10 replicat 1
          83, 88, 102, 88, 67, 71, 85, 70, 65, 70, 82, 69,   #Ech 10 replicat 2
          84, 92, 91, NA, 71, 76, 76, NA, 70, 76, 75, NA)    #Ech 10 replicat 3

Ech11 <- c(82, 91, 91, NA, 81, 90, 90, NA, 79, 85, 85, NA,   #Ech 11 replicat 1
          80, 86, 83, NA, 83, 89, 85, NA, 80, 84, 79, NA,   #Ech 11 replicat 2
          78, 80, 79, NA, 84, 84, 86, NA, 82, 79, 79, NA)   #Ech 11 replicat 3

Ech12 <- c(84, 92, 94, 95, 71, 76, 77, 79, 72, 75, 77, 80,    #Ech 12 replicat 1
          92, 93, 93, 92, 79, 76, 75, 75, 80, 77, 75, 76,   #Ech 12 replicat 2
          89, 90, 92, 105, 73, 73, 74, 85, 72, 72, 74, 86)   #Ech 12 replicat 3

Ech13 <- c(88, 91, 94, NA, 88, 89, 92, NA, 85, 84, 87, NA,   #Ech 13 replicat 1
          83, 85, 88, NA, 86, 87, 89, NA, 82, 81, 82, NA,   #Ech 13 replicat 2
          91, 92, 88, NA, 88, 89, 86, NA, 84, 84, 80, NA)   #Ech 13 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(91, 94, 90, NA, 75, 76, 70, NA, 75, 75, 70, NA)
DMSO <- c(87, 87, 90, 90, 69, 71, 72, 71, 68, 68, 70, 71)
K2CR2O7 <- c(28, 47, 62, 72, 90, 27, 52, 67, 81, 94, 14, 38, 61, 80, 88)

Biolu <- c(Ech10, Ech11, Ech12, Ech13, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech10 <- c(round((100-((Ech10 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech10[I_Ech10<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech11 <- c(round((100-((Ech11 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech11[I_Ech11<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech12 <- c(round((100-((Ech12 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech12[I_Ech12<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech13 <- c(round((100-((Ech13 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech13[I_Ech13<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 

      #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech10, I_Ech11, I_Ech12, I_Ech13, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_EPMO1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_EPMO1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```


```{r Informatisation des données du 2eme set microtox EPMO}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(168) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(4)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("EPMO")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech10", "Ech11", "Ech12", "Ech13", "NaCl", "DMSO")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(24))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(2)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb)/(Ech_nb*3))) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech10 <- c(85, 88, 89, 90, 75, 74, 75, 76, 71, 72, 72, 71,  #Ech 10 replicat 1
          83, 84, 93, 94, 72, 71, 79, 77, 70, 78, 75, 76,  #Ech 10 replicat 2
          87, 92, 94, NA, 75, 80, 77, NA, 74, 78, 78, NA)  #Ech 10 replicat 3

Ech11 <- c(86, 89, 89, NA, 94, 93, 97, NA, 95, 94, 95, NA,   #Ech 11 replicat 1
          85, 89, 79, NA, 95, 98, 92, NA, 98, 98, 92, NA,   #Ech 11 replicat 2
          80, 90, 86, NA, 88, 96, 96, NA, 90, 96, 93, NA)   #Ech 11 replicat 3

Ech12 <- c(95, 90, 87, 92, 82, 79, 75, 79, 83, 78, 74, 78,  #Ech 12 replicat 1
          86, 94, 92, 93, 74, 80, 77, 78, 74, 79, 77, 76,  #Ech 12 replicat 2
          92, 91, 92, 90, 79, 79, 80, 76, 80, 78, 78, 74)  #Ech 12 replicat 3

Ech13 <- c(80, 84, 84, NA, 81, 84, 87, NA, 82, 79, 82, NA,     #Ech 13 replicat 1
          89, 92, 89, NA, 98, 101, 96, NA, 100, 101, 94, NA,   #Ech 13 replicat 2
          82, 95, 88, NA, 93, 108, 100, NA, 95, 109, 98, NA)   #Ech 13 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(96, 94, 87, NA, 82, 78, 74, NA, 82, 78, 72, NA)
DMSO <- c(86, 91, 90, 91, 75, 77, 78, 78, 73, 75, 75, 73)

Biolu <- c(Ech10, Ech11, Ech12, Ech13, NaCl, DMSO) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech10 <- c(round((100-((Ech10 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech10[I_Ech10<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech11 <- c(round((100-((Ech11 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech11[I_Ech11<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech12 <- c(round((100-((Ech12 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech12[I_Ech12<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech13 <- c(round((100-((Ech13 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech13[I_Ech13<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 

      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech10, I_Ech11, I_Ech12, I_Ech13, I_NaCl, I_DMSO) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_EPMO2 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_EPMO2                                                                                   #Change the name of the table
```

```{r Informatisation des données DMSO du 3eme set microtox EPMO}
#Add DMSO control set
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("EPMO")), c(24)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(24))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)),  c(24))  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_bio <- c(rep(NA, 24))    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(3)), 24) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_DMSO, 6)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch                                  
Conc <- c(rep(C_DMSO, 24)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 2)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(65, 78)),4), rep(mean(c(77, 82)), 4), rep(mean(c(79, 79)), 4),
          rep(mean(c(75, 82, 75, 75)),4), rep(mean(c(83, 87, 83, 85)), 4), rep(mean(c(86, 88, 82, 85)), 4))

Biolu <- c(DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch
      
I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                     #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 24)                                       #Do not touch


      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch

#Creation du tableau initial
df_EPMO3 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_EPMO3                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_EPMO0 <- rbind(df_EPMO1, df_EPMO2, df_EPMO3)  #Do not touch
df_EPMO0                                                              #Do not touch

mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_EPMO0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

#Calcul Biolu_pondéré avec le DMSO ramené à 100
df_EPMO <- df_EPMO0  %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% mutate(Biolu_p = round(((Biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100), digits = 3))
df_EPMO

df_EPMO$Ech <- as.character(df_EPMO$Ech)

df_EPMO <- df_EPMO %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "10") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "11") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "12") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "13") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm"
    ))
df_EPMO 
```

```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_EPMO0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_EPMO %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi = 100-round(mean(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi_sd = round(sd(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))#,
                  #"per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2),
                  #"per_Inhi_sd" = round((100-((sd_biolu / mean_DMSO$sd_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech

Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_EPMO0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA)

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_DMSO

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_EPMO0 %>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, per_Inhi_sd)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
df_Nacl_K2

Average_df_EPMO <- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_EPMO

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_EPMO1 <- Average_df_EPMO %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "10") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "11") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "12") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "13") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.33 mm"
    ))
Average_df_EPMO1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_EPMO2 <- Average_df_EPMO %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_EPMO2

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_EPMO3 <- Average_df_EPMO %>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_EPMO3
```

## STATISTIC EPMO

Comparison between : PA 0.60 new / PA 0.60 used / PA 0.33 new / PA 0.33 used / DMSO at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Comparision of variance: 
H0: The fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of EPMO}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30
Stat_EPMO <-  df_EPMO0 %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30")
Stat_EPMO

#H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_EPMO, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_EPMO$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_EPMO$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_EPMO$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

#bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_EPMO) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_EPMO)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho

#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified
```

Conclusion ANOVA:

P < 0.05 H0 rejected, the data support the Ha, the fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


PostHoc Hypothesis: 

0)
New 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

1)
Used 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

8)
New 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

9)
Used 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

2)
New 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

3)
Used 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

4)
New 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

5)
Used 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

6)
New 0.60 vs used 0.60
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

7)
New 0.33 vs Crtl 0.33
H0: The new fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery



```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_EPMO$Biolu, Stat_EPMO$Ech, method="bh")

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey


```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

0) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - New 0.33

1) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - Used 0.33

8) p < 0.05 H0 rejected  
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.33

9) p < 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - New 0.33

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.60

3) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.60

4) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.33

5) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.33

6) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.60

7) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.33 - Used 0.33

```{r Graph %Inhi vs Dilu av. Echs à Tps T fixé}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)

G5 <- Average_df_EPMO1 %>% filter(Tps == "30") %>% ggplot(aes(Dilu, per_Inhi, color = as.factor(Ech))) + geom_line(size = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0.0125, 0.0250, 0.0500, 0.1000)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(min(Average_df_EPMO2$per_Inhi, na.rm = T), max(Average_df_EPMO2$per_Inhi, na.rm = T), by = 2)) +
  labs(title = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence en fonction des concentrations \ndes elutriats de filet de pêche neufs",
       subtitle = "Temps d'exposition 30 minutes",
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Volume (mL) d'élutriat de plastique (DMSO) dans 1 mL de milieu") + 
  scale_colour_manual(name = "Echantillons", values = Ech_color_EPMO) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)))
G5

#_______________________________________________

GK2CR2O7 <- Average_df_EPMO3 %>% filter(Tps == "30") %>% ggplot(aes(Dilu, per_Inhi, color = as.factor(Ech))) + geom_line(size = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0.0625, 0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(round(min(Average_df_EPMO3$per_Inhi, na.rm = T, digits = 0)), max(Average_df_EPMO3$per_Inhi, na.rm = T), by = 5)) +
  labs(title = "Control positif de l'Inhibition de la bioluminescence \ngrâce au Bichromate de potassium (K2CR207)",
       subtitle = "T = 30 minutes",
       y = "Inhibition de la bioluminescence (%)", 
       x = "Volume (mL) de K2CR2O7 dans 1 mL de milieu") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)))
GK2CR2O7

```

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 5min}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN
```

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 5min}
                  Ech11 = "Used gillnet \nn=6",

                  Ech13 = "Used gillnet \nn=6")

G9 <- Average_df_EPMO1 %>% filter(Tps == 30) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Conc))) + 
  geom_col(position = 'dodge', width = 0.5) +  
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin= ifelse(per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd < 0, 0, per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd), ymax=per_Inhi+per_Inhi_sd), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) +
  facet_grid(~ factor(Diam), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.45, 0.45)) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Inhibition as a function of exposure concentration \nto leachate from the English channel gillnet (Polyamide)",
       subtitle = "Solvent: Dymethylsulfoxyde (DMSO) ratio 1g:3ml \nExposition time 30 minutes",
       y = "Average bioluminescence inhibition  (%)", 
       x = "Samples", 
       fill = "Concentration") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Plastic eq. concentration in solvent (mg/mL)", values = Conc_color, guide = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 40, by = 2)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Ech_EPMO) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
G9
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

Color_biolu_EPMO <- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech12" = "aquamarine" , "Ech13" = "#054959", "Ech10" = "aquamarine", "Ech11" = "#054959")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

# compact letter display
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_EPMO)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho

# compact letter display
tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey
# compact letter display
cld_EPMO <- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_EPMO
#Create column FN
FN_EPMO <- c( "Control", "PA γ Ø.60 n", "PA γ Ø.60 u", "PA γ Ø.33 n", "PA γ Ø.33 u")

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_EPMO <- group_by(Stat_EPMO, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_EPMO) %>%
  arrange(desc(w))
dt_EPMO

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
cld_EPMO <- as.data.frame.list(cld_EPMO$Ech)
cld_EPMO
dt_EPMO$cld_EPMO <- cld_EPMO$Letters
dt_EPMO

#X axis
xaxis_EPMO <- c(Ech10 = "PA γ Ø.60 n \nn=6", 
           Ech11 = "PA γ Ø.60 u \n(624h) \nn=6",
           Ech12 = "PA γ Ø.33 n \nn=6",
           Ech13 = "PA γ Ø.33 u \n(624h) \nn=6",
           DMSO = "Control \nn=6")

G <- dt_EPMO %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_EPMO) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_EPMO, labels = xaxis_EPMO) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the leachate of PA γ Ø.60 & PA γ Ø.33",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_EPMO, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 23) = 8.78, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G <- dt_EPMO %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_EPMO) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_EPMO, labels = xaxis_EPMO) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "\n \n",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_EPMO, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(4, 23) = 8.78, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```


# MICROTOX SEABIRD

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er set microtox Seabird}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(183) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(4)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Seabird")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech14", "Ech15", "Ech16", "Ech17", "NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb-15)/(Ech_nb*3)), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech14 <- c(71, 67, 79, NA, 61, 58, 67, NA, 56, 51, 59, NA,    #Ech 14 replicat 1
          68, 75, 74, NA, 60, 70, 66, NA, 55, 58, 57, NA,   #Ech 14 replicat 2
          63, 68, 75, NA, 54, 58, 68, NA, 49, 51, 59, NA)   #Ech 14 replicat 3

# Ech15 <- c(74, 76, 83, NA, 66, 65, 73, NA, 59, 56, 62, NA,    #Ech 15 replicat 1
#           76, 83, 75, NA, 67, 74, 66, NA, 61, 64, 56, NA,  #Ech 15 replicat 2
#           73, 85, 79, NA, 67, 76, 71, NA, 60, 67, 59, NA)    #Ech 15replicat 3

Ech15 <- c(73, 82, 76, NA, 80, 85, 80, NA, 79, 82, 78, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 1
          69, 75, 73, NA, 74, 80, 79, NA, 75, 78, 77, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 2
          70, 71, 78, NA, 75, 77, 81, NA, 76, 76, 79, NA)   #Ech 15 replicat 3

Ech16 <- c(65, 77, 83, NA, 56, 69, 76, NA, 49, 64, 68, NA,    #Ech 16 replicat 1
          64, 72, 79, NA, 54, 64, 72, NA, 48, 57, 64, NA,   #Ech 16 replicat 2
          62, 65, 76, NA, 54, 57, 71, NA, 47, 49, 62, NA)   #Ech 16 replicat 3

# Ech17 <- c(72, 81, 85, NA, 66, 73, 79, NA, 63, 67, 70, NA,    #Ech 17 replicat 1
#           68, 79, 80, NA, 62, 74, 75, NA, 57, 66, 66, NA,  #Ech 17 replicat 2
#           70, 75, 80, NA, 63, 69, 74, NA, 58, 62, 65, NA)    #Ech 17 replicat 3

Ech17 <- c(68, 81, 87, NA, 62, 77, 87, NA, 60, 75, 83, NA,    #Ech 17 replicat 1
          69, 81, 79, NA, 64, 81, 80, NA, 62, 80, 76, NA,  #Ech 17 replicat 2
          76, 72, 79, NA, 74, 71, 81, NA, 65, 72, 77, NA)    #Ech 17 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(84, 84, 94, 92, 71, 73, 84, 80, 62, 64, 73, 70)
DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(87, 92, 93)),4), rep(mean(c(76, 83, 82)), 4), rep(mean(c(71, 75, 73)), 4)) #si pas plusieurs dilutions alors hypothèse que valeur moyenne des DMSO valable pour tous les coeff de dilution de DMSO.
K2CR2O7 <- c(30, 51, 61, 74, 82, 23, 44, 55, 66, 75, 12, 31, 45, 54, 64)

Biolu <- c(Ech14, Ech15, Ech16, Ech17, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

 #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech14 <- c(round((140-((Ech14 / NaCl)*140)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech14[I_Ech14<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech15 <- c(round((100-((Ech15 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech15[I_Ech15<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech16 <- c(round((100-((Ech16 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech16[I_Ech16<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech17 <- c(round((100-((Ech17 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech17[I_Ech17<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 
     
      #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech14, I_Ech15, I_Ech16, I_Ech17, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Seabird1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Seabird1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Informatisation des données du 1er bis set microtox Seabird, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(219) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(5)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Seabird")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech14", "Ech15", "Ech16", "Ech17", "Ech18","NaCl", "DMSO", "K2CR2O7")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12, 15)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(39))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 5), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch
Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625) #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((5*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/5)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3), rep(Dilu_K2CR2O7, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_K2CR2O7 <- c(0)                                   #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((5*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/5)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12), rep(C_K2CR2O7, 15)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch
T_dilu_f5 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 5)    #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 17), rep(T_dilu_f5, 1)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch
length(Tps)
#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech14 <- c(71, 67, 79, NA, 61, 58, 67, NA, 56, 51, 59, NA,    #Ech 14 replicat 1
          68, 75, 74, NA, 60, 70, 66, NA, 55, 58, 57, NA,   #Ech 14 replicat 2
          63, 68, 75, NA, 54, 58, 68, NA, 49, 51, 59, NA)   #Ech 14 replicat 3

Ech18 <- c(74, 76, 83, NA, 66, 65, 73, NA, 59, 56, 62, NA,    #Ech 18 replicat 1
          76, 83, 75, NA, 67, 74, 66, NA, 61, 64, 56, NA,  #Ech 18 replicat 2
          73, 85, 79, NA, 67, 76, 71, NA, 60, 67, 59, NA)    #Ech 18replicat 3

Ech15 <- c(73, 82, 76, NA, 80, 85, 80, NA, 79, 82, 78, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 1
          69, 75, 73, NA, 74, 80, 79, NA, 75, 78, 77, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 2
          70, 71, 78, NA, 75, 77, 81, NA, 76, 76, 79, NA)   #Ech 15 replicat 3

Ech16 <- c(65, 77, 83, NA, 56, 69, 76, NA, 49, 64, 68, NA,    #Ech 16 replicat 1
          64, 72, 79, NA, 54, 64, 72, NA, 48, 57, 64, NA,   #Ech 16 replicat 2
          62, 65, 76, NA, 54, 57, 71, NA, 47, 49, 62, NA)   #Ech 16 replicat 3

# Ech17 <- c(72, 81, 85, NA, 66, 73, 79, NA, 63, 67, 70, NA,    #Ech 17 replicat 1
#           68, 79, 80, NA, 62, 74, 75, NA, 57, 66, 66, NA,  #Ech 17 replicat 2
#           70, 75, 80, NA, 63, 69, 74, NA, 58, 62, 65, NA)    #Ech 17 replicat 3

Ech17 <- c(68, 81, 87, NA, 62, 77, 87, NA, 60, 75, 83, NA,    #Ech 17 replicat 1
          69, 81, 79, NA, 64, 81, 80, NA, 62, 80, 76, NA,  #Ech 17 replicat 2
          76, 72, 79, NA, 74, 71, 81, NA, 65, 72, 77, NA)    #Ech 17 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(84, 84, 94, 92, 71, 73, 84, 80, 62, 64, 73, 70)
DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(87, 92, 93)),4), rep(mean(c(76, 83, 82)), 4), rep(mean(c(71, 75, 73)), 4)) #si pas plusieurs dilutions alors hypothèse que valeur moyenne des DMSO valable pour tous les coeff de dilution de DMSO.
K2CR2O7 <- c(30, 51, 61, 74, 82, 23, 44, 55, 66, 75, 12, 31, 45, 54, 64)

Biolu <- c(Ech14, Ech15, Ech16, Ech17, Ech18, NaCl, DMSO, K2CR2O7) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

 #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech14 <- c(round((140-((Ech14 / NaCl)*140)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech14[I_Ech14<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech15 <- c(round((100-((Ech15 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech15[I_Ech15<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech16 <- c(round((100-((Ech16 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech16[I_Ech16<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech17 <- c(round((100-((Ech17 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech17[I_Ech17<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech18 <- c(round((100-((Ech18 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech18[I_Ech18<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 
     
      #---------------------------#


I_K2CR2O7 <- c(round((100-((K2CR2O7 / Mean_NaCl_f5)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch #Useless

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech14, I_Ech15, I_Ech16, I_Ech17, I_Ech18, I_NaCl, I_DMSO, I_K2CR2O7) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Seabird1 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Seabird1                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```


```{r Informatisation des données du 2eme set microtox Seabird}

#Vecteur Microtox 
row_nb <- c(168) #change the total row number in function of your samples
Ech_nb <- c(4)
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Seabird")), row_nb) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("Ech14", "Ech15", "Ech16", "Ech17", "NaCl", "DMSO")), #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(36, 36, 36, 36, 12, 12)) #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                            de replicat pour cet échantillon. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3 replicats = 36 

#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1, 2, 3)), each = 12) #en fonction du nombre de replicat experimental par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_control <- rep(as.factor(c(NA)), c(24))     #Do not touch
Repli_bio <- c(rep(Repli_ech, 4), Repli_control) #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio) 

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(2)), row_nb) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)          #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)       #Do not touch

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(Dilu_NaCl, 12), rep(Dilu_DMSO, 3)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique) #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                   #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                  #Do not touch

Conc <- c(rep(C_f4, (((4*3)*(3*Ech_nb))/4)), rep(C_NaCl, 12), rep(C_DMSO, 12)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)    #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)   #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, (row_nb)/(Ech_nb*3))) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

Ech14 <- c(70, 80, 89, NA, 70, 80, 90, NA, 67, 75, 82, NA,   #Ech 14 replicat 1
          71, 74, 78, NA, 74, 75, 80, NA, 70, 69, 73, NA,   #Ech 14 replicat 2
          63, 78, 74, NA, 64, 79, 78, NA, 61, 74, 71, NA)   #Ech 14 replicat 3

Ech15 <- c(81, 88, 90, NA, 84, 87, 90, NA, 79, 81, 80, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 1
          79, 87, 97, NA, 84, 92, 104, NA, 81, 83, 96, NA,   #Ech 15 replicat 2
          77, 79, 86, NA, 80, 81, 91, NA, 76, 75, 84, NA)   #Ech 15 replicat 3


Ech16 <- c(71, 74, 78, NA, 64, 72, 78, NA, 64, 75, 79, NA,    #Ech 16 replicat 1
          60, 68, 72, NA, 55, 67, 73, NA, 59, 68, 74, NA,   #Ech 16 replicat 2
          67, 71, 71, NA, 58, 69, 69, NA, 59, 71, 74, NA)   #Ech 16 replicat 3

Ech17 <- c(68, 73, 76, NA, 65, 74, 80, NA, 70, 76, 82, NA,    #Ech 17 replicat 1
          66, 73, 70, NA, 64, 74, 75, NA, 70, 80, 76, NA,  #Ech 17 replicat 2
          60, 70, 78, NA, 58, 75, 79, NA, 61, 79, 82, NA)    #Ech 17 replicat 3

NaCl <- c(95, 93, 96, 95, 91, 88, 96, 96, 91, 89, 99, 98)
DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(77,84,79)), 4), rep(mean(c(81,83,81)), 4), rep(mean(c(86,87,84)), 4)) #si pas plusieurs dilutions alors hypothèse que valeur moyenne des DMSO valable pour tous les coeff de dilution de DMSO

Biolu <- c(Ech14, Ech15, Ech16, Ech17, NaCl, DMSO) # Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

      #Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),  round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions  #Do not touch         
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions      #Do not touch

I_Ech14 <- c(round((140-((Ech14 / NaCl)*140)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech14[I_Ech14<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech15 <- c(round((100-((Ech15 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech15[I_Ech15<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_Ech16 <- c(round((100-((Ech16 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech16[I_Ech16<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch                                    

I_Ech17 <- c(round((100-((Ech17 / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))   #Do not touch
I_Ech17[I_Ech17<0] <- 0                                       #Do not touch

I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                        #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0 
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 12)                                        #Do not touch
 

      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_Ech14, I_Ech15, I_Ech16, I_Ech17, I_NaCl, I_DMSO) #Vecteur final   #Do not touch 

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Seabird2 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Seabird2                                                                                   #Change the name of the table
```

```{r Informatisation des données DMSO du 3eme set microtox Seabird}
#Add DMSO control set
#Vecteur Microtox 
Microtox <- rep(as.factor(c("Seabird")), c(24)) #change your Microtox's name for which you are analyzing the results

#Vecteur nom des echantillons
Ech <- rep(as.factor(c("DMSO")),  #Ajouter le nombre d'échantillons que vous avez testé
           times = c(24))  #Si vous avez Ajouté des échantillons rajouter un chiffre en fonction du nombre                                           de replicat pour cet échantillons. 1 replicats = 12 | 2 replicats = 24 | 3                                                replicats = 36 
Ech
#Vecteurs replicats
Repli_ech <- rep(as.factor(c(1)),  c(24))  #en fonction du nombre de replicat par echantillons ajoutez et ou                                                         enlevez des chiffres
Repli_bio <- c(rep(NA, 24))    #Do not touch
length(Repli_bio)  

Repli_tech <- rep(as.factor(c(3)), 24) #changer le chiffre de droite par la valeur qui vient de s'afficher

#Vecteurs dilution echantillons
Dilu_f4 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)               #Do not touch
#Dilu_f3 <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.025)                     #Do not touch
Dilu_NaCl <- c(1)                                   #Do not touch
Dilu_DMSO <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125)            #Do not touch
#Dilu_K2CR2O7 <- c(1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625)

Dilu <- c(rep(Dilu_DMSO, 6)) #Vecteur final #Do not touch

#Vecteurs concentration equivalent plastique
C_f4 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique, 0.00125*plastique)   #Do not touch
#C_f3 <- c(0.01*plastique, 0.005*plastique, 0.0025*plastique)                     #Do not touch
C_NaCl <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch
C_DMSO <- c(0)                                                                    #Do not touch                                  
Conc <- c(rep(C_DMSO, 24)) #Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs temps microtox
T_dilu_f4 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 4)                               #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f3 <- rep(as.factor(c(5, 15, 30)), each = 3)                              #Do not touch
#T_dilu_f5 <- rep(c(5, 15, 30), each = 5)                                         #Do not touch

Tps <- c(rep(T_dilu_f4, 2)) #Vecteur final                                        #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs Bioluminescence
##Remplissez vous valeurs de bioluminescences. Chaque ligne est un replicat. Respectez l'ordre en rentrant les valeurs de la plus forte dilution à la plus faible. Si que 3 ou 2 dilution écrire "NA". Configuré pour 4 valeurs max par replicats. 
#Ex: val_0.1_T5, val_0.05_T5, val_0.025_T5, val_0.0125_T5, val_0.1_T15, val_0.05_T15, val_0.025_T15, val_0.0125_T15, ...

DMSO <- c(rep(mean(c(84, 88)),4), rep(mean(c(86, 93)), 4), rep(mean(c(85, 91)), 4),
          rep(mean(c(75, 78, 75)),4), rep(mean(c(81, 85, 86)), 4), rep(mean(c(85, 89, 90)), 4)) 

Biolu <- c(DMSO) # Vecteur final         #Do not touch

#Vecteurs valeurs % Inhibition bioluminescence

#Calcul des moyennes des blancs NaCl pour chaque temps de mesure
      Mean_NaCl_f4 <- rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2),    round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 4) #Pour quatre dilutions   #Do not touch
      
      Mean_NaCl_f5 <-  rep(c(round(mean(NaCl[1:3]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[5:7]), digits = 2), round(mean(NaCl[9:11]), digits = 2)), each = 5) #Pour cinq dilutions     #Do not touch
      
I_DMSO <- c(round((100-((DMSO / Mean_NaCl_f4)*100)), digits = 2))                                      #Do not touch
I_DMSO[I_DMSO<0] <- 0
I_NaCl <- rep(0, 24)                                       #Do not touch

      
      #---------------------------#

per_Inhi <- c(I_DMSO) #Vecteur final             #Do not touch

#Creation du tableau initial
df_Seabird3 <- tibble(Microtox, Ech, Repli_bio, Repli_tech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Biolu, per_Inhi) #Change the name of the table
df_Seabird3                                                                                   #Change the name of the table

```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_Seabird <- rbind(df_Seabird1, df_Seabird2, df_Seabird3) %>% 
  mutate(Ech = if_else(Ech == "Ech18", "Ech17", Ech)) #Do not touch
df_Seabird 

df_Seabird$Ech <- as.character(df_Seabird$Ech)

df_Seabird <- df_Seabird %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "14") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "15") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "16") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "17") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
df_Seabird

#Do not touch
```

```{r Tableau Initial}
df_Seabird0 <- rbind(df_Seabird1, df_Seabird2, df_Seabird3)  %>% 
  mutate(Ech = if_else(Ech == "Ech18", "Ech17", Ech)) #Do not touch
df_Seabird0                                                              #Do not touch

mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Seabird0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

#Calcul Biolu_pondéré avec le DMSO ramené à 100
df_Seabird <- df_Seabird0  %>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% mutate(Biolu_p = round(((Biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100), digits = 3))
df_Seabird

df_Seabird$Ech <- as.character(df_Seabird$Ech)

df_Seabird <- df_Seabird %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "14") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "15") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "16") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "17") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
df_Seabird 
```

```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Seabird0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO

Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_Seabird %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi = 100-round(mean(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), 
                              per_Inhi_sd = round(sd(Biolu_p, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))#,
                  #"per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2),
                  #"per_Inhi_sd" = round((100-((sd_biolu / mean_DMSO$sd_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech

Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_Seabird0 %>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA)

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_DMSO

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_Seabird0 %>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA, "per_Inhi_sd" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, per_Inhi_sd)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
df_Nacl_K2

Average_df_Seabird <- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_Seabird

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Seabird1 <- Average_df_Seabird %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "14") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "15") ~ "Outer panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "16") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "17") ~ "Inner panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
Average_df_Seabird1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Seabird2 <- Average_df_Seabird %>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_Seabird2

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_Seabird3 <- Average_df_Seabird %>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_Seabird3
```

## STATISTIC SEABIRD

Comparison between : PA 0.60 new / PA 0.60 used / PA 0.33 new / PA 0.33 used / DMSO at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Comparision of variance: 
H0: The fishing nets don't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of Seabird}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30
Stat_Seabird <-  df_Seabird0 %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30")
Stat_Seabird

#H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_Seabird, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_Seabird$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_Seabird$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_Seabird$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

#bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_Seabird) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_Seabird)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho

#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified

```

Conclusion ANOVA:

P < 0.05 H0 rejected, the data support the Ha, the fishing nets have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery


PostHoc Hypothesis: 

0)
New 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

1)
Used 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

8)
New 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

9)
Used 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

2)
New 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

3)
Used 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

4)
New 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

5)
Used 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

6)
New 0.60 vs used 0.60
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

7)
New 0.33 vs Crtl 0.33
H0: The new fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery



```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_Seabird$Biolu, Stat_Seabird$Ech, method="bh")

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

0) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - New 0.33

1) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - Used 0.33

8) p < 0.05 H0 rejected  
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.33

9) p < 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - New 0.33

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.60

3) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.60

4) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.33

5) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.33

6) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.60

7) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.33 - Used 0.33

```{r Graph % Inhi vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 5min}

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre. 
Ech_seabird <- c(Ech14 = "New gillnet \nn=6", 
                  Ech15 = "Used gillnet \nn=6",
                  Ech16 = "New gillnet \nn=6",
                  Ech17 = "Used gillnet \nn=6")

G9 <- Average_df_Seabird1 %>% filter(Tps == 30, Dilu != 0.0125) %>% ggplot(aes(Ech, per_Inhi, fill = as.factor(Conc))) + 
  geom_col(position = 'dodge', width = 0.5) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin= ifelse(per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd < 0, 0, per_Inhi-per_Inhi_sd), ymax=per_Inhi+per_Inhi_sd), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.5)) +
  facet_grid(~ factor(Diam), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.45, 0.45)) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence Inhibition as a function of exposure concentration \nto leachate from the French gillnet (Polybutylene succinate)",
       subtitle = "Solvent: Dymethylsulfoxyde (DMSO) ratio 1g:3ml \nExposition time 30 minutes",
       y = "Average bioluminescence inhibition (%)", 
       x = "Samples", 
       fill = "Concentration") + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Plastic eq. concentration in solvent (mg/mL)", values = Conc_color, guide = guide_legend(title.position = "top")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 45, by = 2)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = Ech_seabird) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", vjust = 3),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
G9
```
