```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

Color_biolu_Seabird <- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech16" = "aquamarine" , "Ech17" = "#054959", "Ech14" = "aquamarine", "Ech15" = "#054959")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

# compact letter display
#if not parametric
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data= Stat_Seabird) ## P-value < 0.05 
anova(Aov)

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

# compact letter display
cld_Seabird <- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_Seabird

#Create column FN
FN_Seabird <- c( "Control", "PBS δ Ø.60 n", "PBS δ Ø.60 u", "PBS δ Ø.35 n", "PBS δ Ø.35 u")
FN_Seabird

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_Seabird <- group_by(Stat_Seabird, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_Seabird) %>%
  arrange(desc(w))
dt_Seabird

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
cld_Seabird <- as.data.frame.list(cld_Seabird$Ech)
dt_Seabird$cld_Seabird <- cld_Seabird$Letters
dt_Seabird

#X axis
xaxis_Seabird <- c(Ech14 = "PBS δ Ø.60 n \nn=6", 
           Ech15 = "PBS δ Ø.60 u \n(624h)\nn=6",
           Ech16 = "PBS δ Ø.35 n \nn=6",
           Ech17 = "PBS δ Ø.35 u \n(624h)\nn=6",
           DMSO = "Control n=6")

G <- dt_Seabird %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_Seabird) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Seabird, labels = xaxis_Seabird) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the leachate of PBS δ Ø.60 & PBS δ Ø.35",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_Seabird, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G <- dt_Seabird %>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.25, 0.45, 0.45)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = xaxis_Seabird) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Seabird, labels = xaxis_Seabird) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "\n \n",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld_Seabird, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 5)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < 0.001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```

# MICROTOX POLYAMIDES

```{r Table Polyamides}
df_Pasaia0$Ech <- as.character(df_Pasaia0$Ech)
df_Pasaia0 <- df_Pasaia0 %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
df_Pasaia0

df_Arca0$Ech <- as.character(df_Arca0$Ech)
df_Arca0 <- df_Arca0 %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
df_Arca0

df_EPMO0$Ech <- as.character(df_EPMO0$Ech)
df_EPMO0 <- df_EPMO0 %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm"
    ))
df_EPMO0

df_Polyamides <- rbind(df_Pasaia0, df_Arca0, df_EPMO0)
df_Polyamides
```


```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO


Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_Polyamides%>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)), 
                  "per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech


Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = c(rep(0, 36)))

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, Diam)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0

Average_df_Polyamides<- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_Polyamides

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Polyamides1 <- Average_df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO")
Average_df_Polyamides1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_Polyamides2 <- Average_df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_Polyamides2

Average_df_Polyamides4 <- Average_df_Polyamides2 %>% mutate("FN" = rep(as.factor(c("0.35 mm", "0.95 mm", "0.33 mm", "0.60 mm", "0.33 mm", "Control")), times = c(24, 24, 24, 24, 24, 36)))
Average_df_Polyamides4

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_Polyamides3 <- Average_df_Polyamides%>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_Polyamides3
```

## STATISTIC Polyamides

Comparison between : FN manufacturers for Ø0.30 mm at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Here, we have 2 sets of hypotheses:

1)
H0: The FN manufacturer has no effect on the toxicity
HA: The FN manufacturer has an effect on the toxicity


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of Polyamides}
#take just the new FN
Diam_poly <- rep(as.factor(c("Inner", "Control", "Inner", "Control",
                             "Outer", "Inner", "Control", "Outer", "Inner", "Control",
                             "Outer", "Inner", "Control", "Outer", "Inner", "Control")), 
                 times = c(36, 12, 36, 24,  
                           36, 36, 12, 36, 36, 60,
                           36, 36, 12, 36, 36, 36))
Diam_poly

new_polyamides <- df_Polyamides %>% filter(Ech == "Ech1" | Ech == "Ech6" | Ech == "Ech8" | Ech == "Ech10" | Ech == "Ech12" | Ech == "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = Diam_poly)
new_polyamides

used_polyamides <- df_Polyamides %>% filter(Ech == "Ech2" | Ech == "Ech7" | Ech == "Ech9" | Ech == "Ech11" | Ech == "Ech13" | Ech == "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = Diam_poly)
used_polyamides


#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30 & inner panels
Stat_Polyamides<-  used_polyamides %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30", Diam == "Inner" | Diam == "Control") #%>% mutate(Ech = as.factor(Ech))
Stat_Polyamides

 #H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_Polyamides, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_Polyamides$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_Polyamides$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_Polyamides$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_Polyamides) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_Polyamides)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho


#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov))#NON --> P > 0.05 normality verified

```

Conclusion ANOVA:

Here, we have 3 sets of hypotheses:

1) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The FN manufacturer has an effect on the toxicity

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The thread diameter has an effect on the toxicity

3) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The thread diameter has an influence on the FN manufacturer toxicity


PostHoc Hypothesis: 

0)
New 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

1)
Used 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

8)
New 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

9)
Used 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

2)
New 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

3)
Used 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

4)
New 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

5)
Used 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

6)
New 0.60 vs used 0.60
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

7)
New 0.33 vs Crtl 0.33
H0: The new fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery



```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_Polyamides$Biolu, Stat_Polyamides$Ech, method="bh")

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

0) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - New 0.33

1) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - Used 0.33

8) p < 0.05 H0 rejected  
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.33

9) p < 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - New 0.33

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.60

3) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.60

4) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.33

5) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.33

6) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.60

7) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.33 - Used 0.33


## GRAPHIQUES Polyamides

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max ANOVA 1F New FN}

#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30 & inner panels
Stat_Polyamides<-  new_polyamides %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30", Diam == "Inner" | Diam == "Control") #%>% mutate(Ech = as.factor(Ech))
Stat_Polyamides

Color_biolu_Polyamides<- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech1" = "#054959" , "Ech6" = "#054959", "Ech8" = "aquamarine", "Ech10" = "Yellow3", "Ech12" = "#36A7A7")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

# compact letter display
#if not parametric
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data= Stat_Polyamides) ## P-value < 0.05 
anova(Aov)
Aovsummary <- summary.lm(Aov)
r2 <- Aovsummary$r.squared
r2

report(Aov)

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey
plot(TukeyHSD(Aov), las=1)

# compact letter display
cld_Polyamides<- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_Polyamides


#Create column FN
FN_Polyamides<- tibble(Ech = c("DMSO", "Ech1", "Ech8", "Ech12"), FN = c("Control \nn = 12","PA α Ø.35 n \nn = 6", "PA β Ø.33 n \nn = 6", "PA γ Ø.33 n \nn = 6"))
FN_Polyamides

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_Polyamides<- group_by(Stat_Polyamides, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(Ech = FN_Polyamides$Ech, FN = FN_Polyamides$FN, cld = c("a", "bc", "c", "ab"), w = c(80.2, 66.2, 54.7, 76.8), sd = c(10.4, 8.04, 4.75, 12.9)) 
dt_Polyamides

#X axis
xaxis_Polyamides<- c( DMSO = "Control n=12",
           Ech1 = "New gillnet\nn=6", 
           Ech6 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech8 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech10 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech12 = "New gillnet\nn=6")

G <- dt_Polyamides%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  facet_grid(~~fct_relevel(FN,"Control \nn = 12","PA α Ø.35 n \nn = 6","PA β Ø.33 n \nn = 6","PA γ Ø.33 n \nn = 6"), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")  +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Polyamides) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "\n \n",y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 4)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < .001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max ANOVA 1F USED FN}
#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30 & inner panels
Stat_Polyamides<-  used_polyamides %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30", Diam == "Inner" | Diam == "Control") #%>% mutate(Ech = as.factor(Ech))
Stat_Polyamides


Color_biolu_Polyamides<- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech2" = "#054959" , "Ech7" = "#054959", "Ech9" = "aquamarine", "Ech11" = "Yellow3", "Ech13" = "#36A7A7")

#A PART POUR CHANGER LE TITRE OU L'INTERVALLE ENTRE LES VALEURS DE l'AXE "Y" NE TOUCHER A RIEN 

#Regarder en dessous d'abords les graph que vous souhaitez y sont très certainement déja)
#Pour changer le titre modifier le texte vert après le mot "title =". Ne pas oubliez de metre des guillemets au début et à la fin du titre.  

# compact letter display
#if not parametric
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data= Stat_Polyamides) ## P-value < 0.05 
anova(Aov)
Aovsummary <- summary.lm(Aov)
r2 <- Aovsummary$r.squared
r2

report(Aov)

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

# compact letter display
cld_Polyamides<- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_Polyamides


#Create column FN
FN_Polyamides<- c(DMSO = "Control \nn = 12", Ech13 = "PA γ Ø.33 u \nn = 6", Ech2 = "PA α Ø.35 u \nn = 6", Ech9 ="PA β Ø.33 u \nn = 6")
FN_Polyamides

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_Polyamides<- group_by(Stat_Polyamides, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_Polyamides) %>%
  arrange(FN) %>% mutate(cld = c("a", "b", "a", "a"))
dt_Polyamides

# # extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
# cld_Polyamides <- as.data.frame.list(cld_Polyamides$Ech)
# cld <- tibble(cld = cld_Polyamides$Letters)
# dt_Polyamides <- bind_cols(dt_Polyamides, cld) 
# dt_Polyamides

#X axis
xaxis_Polyamides<- c( DMSO = "Control n=12",
           Ech2 = "New gillnet\nn=6", 
           Ech7 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech9 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech11 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech13 = "New gillnet\nn=6")


G <- dt_Polyamides%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  facet_grid(~FN, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")  +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Polyamides) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Average of bioluminescence in function of the \nleachate of the different used polyamide gillnets",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 4)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < .001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G <- dt_Polyamides%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  facet_grid(~FN, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")  +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_Polyamides) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "\n \n",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 4)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < .001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))
```

# MICROTOX PBS_PA

```{r Table PBS_PA}

df_EPMO0$Ech <- as.character(df_EPMO0$Ech)
df_EPMO0 <- df_EPMO0 %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "0") ~ "panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "panel Ø0.33 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "3") ~ "panel Ø0.33 mm",
    ))
df_EPMO0

df_Seabird0$Ech <- as.character(df_Seabird0$Ech)
df_Seabird0 <- df_Seabird0 %>% mutate(Diam = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "4") ~ "panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "5") ~ "panel Ø0.60 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "6") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    endsWith(Ech, "7") ~ "panel Ø0.35 mm",
    ))
df_Seabird0

df_PBS_PA <- rbind(df_EPMO0, df_Seabird0)
df_PBS_PA
```


```{r Moyenne replicat}
#Mac --> Cmd+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"
#Windows --> Crtl+F chunk : Replace "df_samplename" by "df_yoursamplename"

#___________________________________JUST RUN THE CODE | DO NOT TOUCH ANYTHING_____________________________________#
mean_DMSO <- tibble(df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)))
mean_DMSO


Average_Ech <-  tibble(df_PBS_PA%>% filter(grepl("Ech", Ech)) %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)), 
                  "per_Inhi" = round((100-((mean_biolu / mean_DMSO$mean_biolu)*100)), digits = 2))

Average_Ech[Average_Ech<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0
Average_Ech


Average_DMSO <- tibble(df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech == "DMSO") %>% 
                    group_by(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, Diam) %>% 
                    summarise(mean_biolu = round(mean(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2), sd_biolu = round(sd(Biolu, na.rm=TRUE), digits = 2)),
                    "per_Inhi" = c(rep(0, 24)))

Average_DMSO[Average_DMSO<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0

df_Nacl_K2 <- df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech == "NaCl" | Ech == "K2CR2O7") %>% mutate("mean_biolu" = Biolu, "sd_biolu" = NA) %>% dplyr::select(Microtox, Ech, Tps, Dilu, Conc, mean_biolu, sd_biolu, per_Inhi, Diam)

df_Nacl_K2[df_Nacl_K2<0] <- 0 #pour mettre toutes les valeurs negatives à 0

Average_df_PBS_PA<- rbind(Average_Ech, Average_DMSO, df_Nacl_K2)
Average_df_PBS_PA

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_PBS_PA1 <- Average_df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl" & Ech != "DMSO")
Average_df_PBS_PA1

#Table_mean without K2CR2O7
Average_df_PBS_PA2 <- Average_df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech != "K2CR2O7" & Ech != "NaCl")
Average_df_PBS_PA2

Average_df_PBS_PA4 <- Average_df_PBS_PA2 %>% mutate("FN" = rep(as.factor(c("0.60 mm", "0.33 mm", "0.60 mm", "0.35 mm", "Control")), times = c(24, 24, 24, 24, 24)))
Average_df_PBS_PA4

#Table_K2CR2O7
Average_df_PBS_PA3 <- Average_df_PBS_PA%>% filter(Ech == "K2CR2O7")
Average_df_PBS_PA3
```

## STATISTIC PBS_PA

Comparison between : FN manufacturers for Ø0.30 mm at maximum concentration and at 30 min of exposure:

Here, we have 2 sets of hypotheses:

1)
H0: The FN manufacturer has no effect on the toxicity
HA: The FN manufacturer has an effect on the toxicity


```{r Test Stats significante differences between simple gillnet of PBS_PA}
#take just the new FN
Diam_poly <- rep(as.factor(c("Outer", "Inner", "Control", "Outer", "Inner", "Control",
                             "Outer", "Inner", "Control", "Outer", "Inner", "Control")), 
                 times = c(36, 36, 12, 36, 36, 36,
                           36, 36, 12, 36, 36, 36))
Diam_poly

new_PBS_PA <- df_PBS_PA %>% filter(Ech == "Ech10" | Ech == "Ech12" | Ech == "Ech14" | Ech == "Ech16" | Ech == "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = Diam_poly)
new_PBS_PA

used_PBS_PA <- df_PBS_PA %>% filter(Ech == "Ech11" | Ech == "Ech13" | Ech == "Ech15" | Ech == "Ech17" | Ech == "DMSO") %>% mutate(Diam = Diam_poly)
used_PBS_PA


#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30 & inner panels
Stat_PBS_PA<-  new_PBS_PA %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") #%>% mutate(Ech = as.factor(Ech))
Stat_PBS_PA

 #H0: The data are sampled from a population having a normal distribution
#Ha: The data are sampled from a population not having a normal

ggplot(Stat_PBS_PA, aes(x=Biolu)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5)
qqnorm(Stat_PBS_PA$Biolu, pch = 16)
qqline(Stat_PBS_PA$Biolu) 
shapiro.test(Stat_PBS_PA$Biolu) # P-value > 0.05 --> normal distributed

#H0 : les variances des deux groupes sont égales.
#Ha : les variances sont différentes.

bartlett.test(Biolu ~ Ech, data=Stat_PBS_PA) #P-value > 0.05 --> equality of variance

#Anova
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data = Stat_PBS_PA)
anova(Aov) # < 0.05 --> On rejète Ho


#Analyses Résidus
summary.lm(Aov)
plot(Aov) #--> Normalité ok
shapiro.test(residuals(Aov)) #--> P > 0.05 normality verified

```

Conclusion ANOVA:

Here, we have 3 sets of hypotheses:

1) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The FN manufacturer has an effect on the toxicity

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The thread diameter has an effect on the toxicity

3) p < 0.05 H0 rejected
HA: The thread diameter has an influence on the FN manufacturer toxicity


PostHoc Hypothesis: 

0)
New 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

1)
Used 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

8)
New 0.60 vs Used 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

9)
Used 0.60 vs New 0.33
H0: The larger and the wider fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The larger and the wider fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

2)
New 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

3)
Used 0.60 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

4)
New 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The 0.60 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The 0.60 fishing reduce the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

5)
Used 0.33 vs Crtl
H0: The New 0.33 fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The New 0.33 fishing net has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

6)
New 0.60 vs used 0.60
H0: The new and the used fishing net don't have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery
Ha: The new and the used fishing net have a different effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery

7)
New 0.33 vs Crtl 0.33
H0: The new fishing net doesn't have an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery 
Ha: The new fishing has an effect on the bio luminescence of the bacteria allivibrio fischery



```{r Post Hoc}
#Post Hoc
#dunnTest(Stat_PBS_PA$Biolu, Stat_PBS_PA$Ech, method="bh")

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey

```
Conclusion Post-Hoc:

0) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - New 0.33

1) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - Used 0.33

8) p < 0.05 H0 rejected  
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.33

9) p < 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Used 0.60 - New 0.33

2) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.60

3) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.60

4) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - New 0.33

5) p > 0.05 H0 cannot be rejected 
No Significative difference of bio luminescence between Crtl - Used 0.33

6) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.60 - Used 0.60

7) p < 0.05 H0 rejected 
Significative difference of bio luminescence between New 0.33 - Used 0.33


## GRAPHIQUES PBS_PA

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max ANOVA 1F New FN}

#Table Dilu = 0.1 & Tps = 30 & inner panels
Stat_PBS_PA<-  new_PBS_PA %>% filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") #%>% mutate(Ech = as.factor(Ech))
Stat_PBS_PA

Color_biolu_PBS_PA<- c("DMSO" = "#A48A7B", "Ech10" = "#054959" , "Ech12" = "#36A7A7", "Ech14" = "olivedrab3", "Ech16" = "olivedrab1")

# compact letter display
#if not parametric
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data= Stat_PBS_PA) ## P-value < 0.05 
anova(Aov)
Aovsummary <- summary.lm(Aov)
r2 <- Aovsummary$r.squared
r2

report(Aov)

tukey <- TukeyHSD(Aov)
tukey
plot(TukeyHSD(Aov), las=1)

# compact letter display
cld_PBS_PA<- multcompLetters4(Aov, tukey)
cld_PBS_PA

#Create column FN
FN_PBS_PA<- tibble(Ech = c("DMSO", "Ech10", "Ech12", "Ech14", "Ech16"), 
                   FN = c("Control \nn = 12", 
                          "PA γ Ø.60 n \nn = 6", "PA γ Ø.33 n \nn = 6", 
                          "PBS δ Ø.60 n \nn = 6", "PBS δ Ø.35 n \nn = 6" ))
FN_PBS_PA

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_PBS_PA<- group_by(Stat_PBS_PA, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_PBS_PA$FN, cld = c("a", "a", "ab", "bc", "c"))
dt_PBS_PA

#X axis
xaxis_PBS_PA<- c( DMSO = "Control n=12",
           Ech1 = "New gillnet\nn=6", 
           Ech6 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech8 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech10 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech12 = "New gillnet\nn=6")

G <- dt_PBS_PA%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  facet_grid(~~fct_relevel(FN,"Control \nn = 12", 
                          "PA γ Ø.60 n \nn = 6", "PBS δ Ø.60 n \nn = 6", "PA γ Ø.33 n \nn = 6" 
                          , "PBS δ Ø.35 n \nn = 6" ), 
             scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")  +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_PBS_PA) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the \nleachate of the different new PA γ & PBS δ",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 4)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < .001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 12, 
               hjust = 0.97, 
               vjust = 199, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

G <- dt_PBS_PA%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
```

```{r Graph Biolu vs Ech av. Dilu à Tps 30min Conc max ANOVA 1F Used FN}
# compact letter display
Aov <- aov(Biolu ~ Ech, data= Stat_PBS_PA) ## P-value < 0.05

r2 <- Aovsummary$r.squared
r2

                   FN = c("Control \nn = 12",

                          "PBS δ Ø.60 u \nn = 6", "PBS δ Ø.35 u \nn = 6" ))
FN_PBS_PA

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
dt_PBS_PA<- group_by(Stat_PBS_PA, Ech) %>%
  summarise(w=mean(Biolu, na.rm = T), sd = sd(Biolu, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = FN_PBS_PA$FN, cld = c("a", "a", "a", "a", "b"))
dt_PBS_PA

#X axis
xaxis_PBS_PA<- c( DMSO = "Control n=12",
           Ech11 = "New gillnet\nn=6", 
           Ech13 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech15 = "New gillnet\nn=6",
           Ech17 = "New gillnet\nn=6")

G <- dt_PBS_PA%>% ggplot(aes(x=Ech, y=w)) + 
  geom_bar(aes(fill=Ech), stat="identity", position="dodge2", width = 0.5, show.legend = T) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=w-sd,ymax=w+sd), width=0.05, position=position_dodge(.3)) +
  facet_grid(~~fct_relevel(FN,"Control \nn = 12", 
                          "PA γ Ø.60 u \nn = 6", "PBS δ Ø.60 u \nn = 6", "PA γ Ø.33 u \nn = 6" 
                          , "PBS δ Ø.35 u \nn = 6" ), 
             scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")  +
  force_panelsizes(cols = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_biolu_PBS_PA) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence average according to the \nleachate of the different new PA γ & PBS δ",
       subtitle = "Exposition time 30 minutes \nPlastic equivalent concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml",
       y = "Average of bioluminescence", 
       x = "Samples")  +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", size = 0.1),
        legend.position="none",
        legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm')) +
  geom_text(aes(Ech, label = cld, y = w + sd), vjust = -1, size = 4)
G

G + labs(caption = "ANOVA 1 factor: \nF(3, 25) = 20.09, p < .001 \nPostHoc test: Tukey HSD") + theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif",
```

# Results Publi DMSO 
```{r full table}

df_St_Jean$Ech <- as.character(df_St_Jean$Ech)
df_St_Jean_n <- df_St_Jean %>% mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("Ech", Ech), "hppe_e425", 
                                                 if_else(grepl("DMSO", Ech), "dmso",NA)),
                                    state = case_when( endsWith(Ech, "3") ~ "used", 
                                                       endsWith(Ech, "4") ~ "new",
                                                       endsWith(Ech, "5") ~ "used+"))
df_St_Jean_n


df_Pasaia0$Ech <- as.character(df_Pasaia0$Ech)
df_Pasaia0_n <- df_Pasaia0 %>% mutate(FN = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "pa_a35",
    endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "pa_a35",
    endsWith(Ech, "DMSO") ~ "dmso",),
    state = case_when( endsWith(Ech, "1") ~ "new", 
                       endsWith(Ech, "2") ~ "used"))
df_Pasaia0_n


df_Arca0$Ech <- as.character(df_Arca0$Ech)
df_Arca0_n <- df_Arca0 %>% mutate(FN = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "6") ~ "pa_b95",
    endsWith(Ech, "7") ~ "pa_b95",
    endsWith(Ech, "8") ~ "pa_b33",
    endsWith(Ech, "9") ~ "pa_b33",
    endsWith(Ech, "DMSO") ~ "dmso"),    
    state = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "6") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "7") ~ "used",
    endsWith(Ech, "8") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "9") ~ "used"
    ))
df_Arca0_n

df_EPMO0$Ech <- as.character(df_EPMO0$Ech)
df_EPMO0_n <- df_EPMO0 %>% mutate(FN = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "10") ~ "pa_g60",
    endsWith(Ech, "11") ~ "pa_g60",
    endsWith(Ech, "12") ~ "pa_g33",
    endsWith(Ech, "13") ~ "pa_g33",
    endsWith(Ech, "DMSO") ~ "dmso",
    )) %>% 
  mutate(state = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "10") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "11") ~ "used",
    endsWith(Ech, "12") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "13") ~ "used"))
df_EPMO0_n


df_Seabird0$Ech <- as.character(df_Seabird0$Ech)
df_Seabird0_n <- df_Seabird0 %>% 
  mutate(FN = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "14") ~ "pbs_d60",
    endsWith(Ech, "15") ~ "pbs_d60",
    endsWith(Ech, "16") ~ "pbs_d35",
    endsWith(Ech, "17") ~ "pbs_d35",
    endsWith(Ech, "DMSO") ~ "dmso",),
    state = case_when(
    endsWith(Ech, "14") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "15") ~ "used",
    endsWith(Ech, "16") ~ "new",
    endsWith(Ech, "17") ~ "used"))
df_Seabird0_n


df_all <- bind_rows(df_St_Jean_n, df_Pasaia0_n, df_Arca0_n, df_EPMO0_n, df_Seabird0_n) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(FN)) %>% 
  mutate(rep = if_else(Repli_bio == "1" & Repli_tech == "2", "4",
                       if_else(Repli_bio == "2" & Repli_tech == "2", "5",
                               if_else(Repli_bio == "3" & Repli_tech == "2", "6", Repli_bio)))) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-Diam, -Ech, -Microtox, -Repli_bio, -Repli_tech) 
df_all
```

```{r Statistic multivariées, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

df_stat3 <- df_all %>% 
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30", !(is.na(per_Inhi))) %>%
  unite(FN_state, FN, state, sep = "_", remove = F)

df_stat3

#test ANOVA
#H0= toute les moyennes sont égales
#H1= au moins une des moyennes est différente des autres

res <- aov(per_Inhi ~ FN_state, data = df_stat3)
summary(res)

#H0 = distribution normale 
#H1 = pas de distribution normale
shapiro.test(res$residuals) #p-value > 0.05 donc H0 cannot be rejected 

KW <- kruskal.test(per_Inhi ~ FN_state, data = df_stat3)
KW

MWU_res_n <- PMCMRplus::kwAllPairsDunnTest(per_Inhi ~ as.factor(FN_state), df_stat3, p.adjust.method = "holm")
MWU_res_n

#Letters

df_pval =  MWU_res_n$p.value
df_pval

df_pval2 = fullPTable(df_pval)
df_pval2

Lett <- multcompLetters(df_pval2)
Wlett <- as.vector(unlist(Lett$Letters))

label_n <- data.frame(label = Wlett)
label_n

max_values <- New %>%
  group_by(FN) %>%
  summarize(max_value = max(aju_HR, na.rm = F))
max_values

Star_dmso <- as_tibble(df_pval2, rownames = "FN") %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, dmso) %>% 
  mutate(diff = if_else(dmso == 1 & FN == "dmso", NA,
                        if_else(dmso >= 0.05, "ns",
                                if_else(dmso < 0.0001, "****",
                                        if_else(dmso < 0.001, "***",
                                                if_else(dmso < 0.01, "**",
                                                      if_else(dmso < 0.05, "*", NA)))))))
Star_dmso

MWU_n_Lett_New <- cbind(max_values, label_n, diff = Star_dmso$diff, state = c("new"))
MWU_n_Lett_New

```


```{r statistics New vs Used}
normality <- df_all %>%
  filter(FN != "pa_g33",
         FN != "pa_g60" | state != "used",
         FN != "pa_b33" | state != "used") %>%
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") %>% 
  group_by(FN, state) %>%
  summarise(p_value = shapiro.test(per_Inhi)$p.value)
normality

df_all %>%  
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = per_Inhi, fill = FN)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  geom_density() +
  facet_wrap(FN~state, scales = "free_y", nrow = 2)

#No normality of all the data. --> Use of a non-parametric test for independent two-sample comparison of mean Mann-U test
# Diviser le dataframe par FN
df_list <- df_all %>%
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") %>%
  filter(FN != "dmso") %>% 
  group_split(FN)

results <- list()

for (i in seq_along(df_list)) {
  df_sub <- df_list[[i]]
  states <- unique(df_sub$state)
  for (j in 1:(length(states) - 1)) {
    for (k in (j + 1):length(states)) {
      sample1 <- df_sub[df_sub$state == states[j], "per_Inhi"]$per_Inhi
      sample2 <- df_sub[df_sub$state == states[k], "per_Inhi"]$per_Inhi
      test_result <- wilcox.test(sample1, sample2, paired = FALSE)
      result <- tibble(
        FN = df_sub$FN[1],
        vs = paste(states[j], states[k], sep = "_vs_"),
        p_value = test_result$p.value,
        W = test_result$statistic)
      results <- c(results, list(result))}}}

results_MU <- bind_rows(results) %>% 
  mutate(p_value = if_else(is.na(p_value), 0, p_value)) %>%  
  na.omit() %>% 
  separate(vs, into = c("state1", "state2"), sep = "_vs_", remove = T) %>% 
  mutate(L1 = "a",
         L2 = if_else(p_value < 0.05, "b", "a")) %>% 
  mutate(diff = if_else(p_value < 0.0001, "****", 
                        if_else(p_value < 0.001, "***",
                                if_else(p_value < 0.01, "**",
                                        if_else(p_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))))
results_MU

r1 <- results_MU %>% dplyr::select(FN, state1, p_value, W, L1, diff) %>% rename(state = state1, lett = L1)
r2 <- results_MU %>% dplyr::select(FN, state2, p_value, W, L2, diff) %>% rename(state = state2, lett = L2)

res_sign1 <- bind_rows(r1, r2) %>% 
  mutate(lett = if_else(FN == "pa_g33", NA, lett)) %>% 
  arrange(FN) %>% 
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA \u03B3 60",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA \u03B3 33",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS \u03B4 60",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS \u03B4 35",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "HPPE \u03B5 4.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA \u03B2 95",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA \u03B2 33",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA \u03B1 35", 
                                      if_else(grepl("dmso", FN), "DMSO\ncontrol", FN))))))))))
res_sign1

res_sign <- res_sign1[4:20,]
res_sign

```

Dans le test de Mann-Whitney U, la statistique W est la somme des rangs des observations dans l'un des groupes. La taille de W dépend de la taille des échantillons et de la disposition des données.
Une valeur plus élevée de W indique généralement une plus grande différence de position entre les groupes, ce qui suggère des preuves plus solides contre l'hypothèse nulle selon laquelle les distributions des deux groupes sont identiques.

```{r statistic Blank vs FN}

df_stat <- df_all %>% 
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30", FN == "dmso", !(is.na(per_Inhi))) %>% 
  slice(rep(1:n(), each = 2)) %>%
  mutate(state = rep(c("new", "used"), n()/2)) %>% 
  arrange(state)
df_stat

df_stat2 <- bind_rows(df_all %>%  filter(FN != "dmso"), df_stat)
df_stat2

# Créer une liste pour stocker les résultats
results <- list()

# Boucle sur chaque temps d'exposition
unique_times <- unique(df_stat2$state)

for (t in unique_times) {
  # Filtrer les données pour le temps t
  control_data <- df_stat2 %>%
    filter(FN == "dmso", state == t) %>%
    pull(per_Inhi)
  
  treatment_data <- df_stat2 %>%
    filter(FN != "dmso", state == t)
  
  # Boucle sur chaque traitement (sauf BLC)
  unique_treatments <- unique(treatment_data$FN)
  
  for (treatment in unique_treatments) {
    treatment_values <- treatment_data %>%
      filter(FN == treatment) %>%
      pull(per_Inhi)
    
    # Vérifier qu'il y a suffisamment de données dans les deux groupes
    if (length(control_data) > 1 && length(treatment_values) > 1) {
      # Effectuer le test de Wilcoxon
      test_result <- wilcox.test(control_data, treatment_values)
      
      # Stocker les résultats dans la liste
      results[[paste(treatment, t, sep = "_")]] <- test_result$p.value
    } else {
      # Si pas assez de données, stocker NA dans la liste
      results[[paste(treatment, t, sep = "_")]] <- NA
    }
  }
}

# Afficher les résultats
results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(x) {
  data.frame(Treatment_Time = x, P_Value = results[[x]])
}))

results

results_MU1 <- bind_rows(results) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "FN", values_to = "p_value") %>%
  separate(FN, into = c("FN", "time"), sep = "(?<=[0-9])_", remove = T) %>% 
  mutate(diff = if_else(p_value < 0.0001, "####", 
                        if_else(p_value < 0.001, "###",
                                if_else(p_value < 0.01, "##",
                                        if_else(p_value < 0.05, "#", "ns")))),
         diff = if_else(is.na(diff), "ns", diff)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA \u03B360",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA \u03B333",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS \u03B460",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS \u03B435",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "PE-PP \u03B54.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA \u03B295",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA \u03B233",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA \u03B135", 
                                      if_else(grepl("dmso", FN), "DMSO\ncontrol", FN)))))))))) %>% 
  rename(state = time)
results_MU1

```

```{r Graph New vs Used barplot}
G_df_fin <- df_avg %>%  
  filter(state != "used" | FN != "hppe_e425") %>%
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA \u03B360",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA \u03B333",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS \u03B460",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS \u03B435",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "PE-PP \u03B54.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA \u03B295",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA \u03B233",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA \u03B135", 
                                      if_else(grepl("dmso", FN), "DMSO\ncontrol", FN))))))))),
         state = if_else(state == "used+", "used", state),
         state = if_else(state == "used", "EOL", state)) %>% 
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("new", "EOL"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = FN, y = mean, group=state, fill = FN, pattern = state)) +
  geom_col_pattern(width = 0.5, position = "dodge", pattern_density = 0.1, 
                   pattern_fill = "white", pattern_color = "white", pattern_spacing = 0.015, show.legend = T) +
  scale_pattern_discrete(na.value = "none", choices = c("new" = "none", "EOL" = "stripe"), 
                         limits = c("new", "EOL")) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.1, linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN5, guide = "none")+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 43), breaks = seq(0, 43, by = 5)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Bioluminescence inhibition of the new and used fishing nets' extracts",
       #subtitle = "Exposure time 30 minutes \nMesoplastic eq. concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml\nDMSO dilution 1/100",
       x = "Fishing nets",
       y = "Bioluminescence inhibition (%)",
       pattern = "FN State") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(2)),
        legend.position = "right",
        legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.3, 'cm'),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold', color = "black"),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.85, "cm"),
        legend.key.width = unit(1, "cm"),
        legend.title.align = 0,
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, -1, 1), "cm")) +
  guides(pattern = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))
G_df_fin

G_df_fin + 
  # geom_text(data = res_sign, 
  #           aes(x = state, y = 50, label = lett),
  #           size = 6, 
  #           vjust = -1,

  geom_text(data = results_MU1 %>% mutate(diff = if_else(diff == "ns", NA, diff)), aes(x = FN, y = 41, label = diff),
            size = 4, vjust = -1, color="red3", fontface = "bold",
            position = position_dodge(width=0.6),
            show.legend = FALSE,
            na.rm = F) +
  geom_bracket(aes(xmin = 1.88 , xmax= 2.12, 
                           y.position = 36,
                           label = "**"),
                       size = 1.2,
               label.size = 7,
                       data = subset(res_sign, FN == "PA α 35"),
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_bracket(aes(xmin = 2.88 , xmax= 3.12, 
                           y.position = 36,
                           label = "**"),
                       size = 1.2,
               label.size = 7,
                       data = subset(res_sign, FN == "PA β 33"),
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_bracket(aes(xmin = 3.88 , xmax= 4.12, 
                           y.position = 36,
                           label = "**"),
                       size = 1.2,
               label.size = 7,
                       data = subset(res_sign, FN == "PA β 95"),
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_bracket(aes(xmin = 6.88 , xmax= 7.12, 
                           y.position = 36,
                           label = "*"),
                       size = 1.2,
               label.size = 7,
                       data = subset(res_sign, FN == "PBS δ 35"),
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_bracket(aes(xmin = 7.88 , xmax= 8.12, 
                           y.position = 38,

                       size = 1.2,
               label.size = 7,
                       data = subset(res_sign, FN == "PBS δ 60"),
               inherit.aes = FALSE) #+

```


```{r statistics PA}
df_PA <- df_all %>% filter(grepl("pa", FN))
df_PA

normality <- df_PA %>%
  filter(FN != "pa_g33", 
         FN != "pa_g60" | state != "used", 
         FN != "pa_b33" | state != "used", 
         FN != "pa_b95" | state != "used") %>% 
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") %>% 
  group_by(FN, state) %>%
  summarise(p_value = shapiro.test(per_Inhi)$p.value)
normality

df_PA %>%  
  filter(Dilu == 1) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = per_Inhi, fill = FN)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  geom_density() +
  facet_wrap(FN~state, scales = "free_y", nrow = 2)

#No normality of all the data. --> Use of a non-parametric test for independent two-sample comparison of mean Mann-U test
#------------KW-----------------#

df_PA_new <- df_PA %>% 
  filter(state == "new", Dilu == 1, Tps == 30)
df_PA_new

#condition non respectee donc test de kruskal
kruskal.test(per_Inhi ~ FN, df_PA_new)

#P-value < 0.05, on rejette H0,au moins une des moyennes est significativement différente
MWU_res_n <- kwAllPairsDunnTest(per_Inhi ~ as.factor(FN), df_PA_new, p.adjust.method = "holm")
MWU_res_n

df_pval =  MWU_res_n$p.value
df_pval

df_pval2 = fullPTable(df_pval)
df_pval2

Lett <- multcompLetters(df_pval2)
Wlett <- as.vector(unlist(Lett$Letters))

label_n <- data.frame(label = Wlett)
label_n

max_values <- df_PA_new %>%
  group_by(FN) %>%
  summarise(max_val = 1.5 * IQR(per_Inhi, na.rm = TRUE)+1)
max_values

# Star_ctl <- as_tibble(df_pval2, rownames = "FN") %>%
#   dplyr::select(FN, blc) %>%
#   mutate(diff = if_else(blc == 1 & FN == "blc", NA,
#                         if_else(blc >= 0.05, "ns",
#                                 if_else(blc < 0.0001, "****",
#                                         if_else(blc < 0.001, "***",
#                                                 if_else(blc < 0.01, "**",
#                                                       if_else(blc < 0.05, "*", NA)))))))
# Star_ctl

MWU_PA_n <- cbind(max_values, label_n) %>% mutate(state = "new")
MWU_PA_n

df_PA_used <- df_PA %>% 
  filter(state == "used", Dilu == 1, Tps == 30)
df_PA_used

#condition non respectee donc test de kruskal
kruskal.test(per_Inhi ~ FN, df_PA_used)

#P-value < 0.05, on rejette H0,au moins une des moyennes est significativement différente
MWU_res_n <- kwAllPairsDunnTest(per_Inhi ~ as.factor(FN), df_PA_used, p.adjust.method = "holm")
MWU_res_n

df_pval =  MWU_res_n$p.value
df_pval

df_pval2 = fullPTable(df_pval)
df_pval2

Lett <- multcompLetters(df_pval2)
Wlett <- as.vector(unlist(Lett$Letters))

label_n <- data.frame(label = Wlett)
label_n

max_values <- df_PA_used %>%
  group_by(FN) %>%
  summarise(max_val = 1.5 * IQR(per_Inhi, na.rm = TRUE)+1)
max_values

MWU_PA_u <- cbind(max_values, label_n) %>% mutate(state = "used")
MWU_PA_u

MWU_PA <- bind_rows(MWU_PA_n, MWU_PA_u) %>% 
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA6 \u03B3 60",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA6 \u03B3 33",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS-PBAT \u03B4 60",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS-PBAT \u03B4 35",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "HPPE-PP \u03B5 4.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA6 \u03B2 95",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA6 \u03B2 33",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA6 \u03B1 35", 
                                      if_else(grepl("dmso", FN), "Solvent control", FN))))))))))
MWU_PA
```

Dans le test de Mann-Whitney U, la statistique W est la somme des rangs des observations dans l'un des groupes. La taille de W dépend de la taille des échantillons et de la disposition des données.
Une valeur plus élevée de W indique généralement une plus grande différence de position entre les groupes, ce qui suggère des preuves plus solides contre l'hypothèse nulle selon laquelle les distributions des deux groupes sont identiques.

```{r Graph PA}
G_df_PA <- df_PA %>% 
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA6 \u03B3 60",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA6 \u03B3 33",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS-PBAT \u03B4 60",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS-PBAT \u03B4 35",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "HPPE-PP \u03B5 4.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA6 \u03B2 95",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA6 \u03B2 33",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA6 \u03B1 35", 
                                      if_else(grepl("dmso", FN), "Solvent control", FN)))))))))) %>% 
  filter(Dilu == 1, Tps == "30") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = FN, y = per_Inhi, fill = FN)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, 
               geom = "point", 
               size = 2, 
               color = "red3", 
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 56)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN4)+
  facet_wrap(~state, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
  labs(title = "Bioluminescence inhibition of the new and used \nPA6 fishing nets' leachates",
       subtitle = "Exposure time 30 minutes \nMesoplastic eq. concentration in solvent 3,3 mg/ml\nDMSO dilution 1/100",
       x = "Fishing nets",
       y = "Bioluminescence inhibition (%)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 20), color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 3, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold'),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(2)),
        legend.position = "none",
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm'),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12, face = 'bold', color = "black"),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.85, "cm"),
        legend.key.width = unit(1, "cm"),
        legend.title.align = 0,
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, -1, 1), "cm"))
G_df_PA

G_df_PA + 
  geom_text(data = MWU_PA, 
            aes(x = FN, y = 50, label = label),
            size = 6, 
            vjust = -1, 
            color="black",
            show.legend = FALSE,
            na.rm = T) +
  labs(caption = "Kruskal Wallis test with holm correction: \nnew: p < 0.05 \nused: p < 0.05\nPostHoc test: Dunn test\nn = 6") + 
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(family = "serif", 
               face = "bold", 
               color = 1, 
               size = 14, 
               hjust = 0.99, 
               vjust = 175, 
               angle = 0, 
               lineheight = 1))

```

Une valeur plus élevée de W indique généralement une plus grande différence de position entre les groupes, ce qui suggère des preuves plus solides contre l'hypothèse nulle selon laquelle les distributions des deux groupes sont identiques.

```{r Automatic EC50 EC20 EC10}

library(drc)

# Step 1: Create a dataframe with dmso values for Conc = 0
dmso_values <- df_all %>%
  filter(FN == "dmso") %>%
  mutate(Dilu = 0) %>% 
  filter(Dilu == 0, Tps == "30") %>% 
  dplyr::select(Dilu, per_Inhi) %>% 
  na.omit()
dmso_values

# Step 2: Get unique FN values excluding dmso
fn_values <- unique(df_all$FN[df_all$FN != "dmso"])

# Step 3: Create new rows for each FN with Conc = 0
new_rows <- crossing(FN = fn_values, dmso_values)


# Define the custom function with error handling
process_fn_state <- function(data) {
  # Filter data for Tps == "30"
  filtered_data <- data %>% filter(Tps == "30")
  
  # Check if there are enough non-NA values
  if (sum(!is.na(filtered_data$per_Inhi)) < 4 || sum(!is.na(filtered_data$Dilu)) < 4) {
    return(tibble(model_summary = list("Insufficient non-NA data"),
                  EC_values = list("Insufficient non-NA data")))
  }
  
  # Fit the model
  tryCatch({
    model <- drm(per_Inhi ~ Dilu, data = filtered_data, 
                 fct = LL.4(fixed=c(NA, 0, 100, NA), 
                            names = c("Slope", "Lower Limit", "Upper Limit", "ED50")))
    
    # Calculate EC values
    EC_values <- ED(model, c(20, 10), interval = "delta")
    
    # Return a tibble with model summary and EC values
    tibble(model_summary = list(summary(model)),
           EC_values = list(EC_values))
  }, error = function(e) {
    tibble(model_summary = list(paste("Error:", e$message)),
           EC_values = list("Error in calculation"))
  })
}

##CEx new-----------------# 

# Step 4: Add these new rows to the original dataframe
df_all_ECx_new <- bind_rows(df_all, new_rows) %>%
  arrange(FN, Dilu) %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "new", state),
         Tps = if_else(is.na(Tps), "30", Tps)) %>%
  filter(state != "used+") %>% 
  filter(FN != "dmso", Tps == "30")
df_all_ECx_new

# Apply the function to each combination of FN and state
results <- df_all_ECx_new %>%
  filter(state == "new") %>%
  group_by(FN, state) %>%
  group_modify(~process_fn_state(.x))
results

# Access specific results
successful_results <- results %>%
  filter(!map_lgl(model_summary, ~is.character(.x[[1]])))
successful_results

ec_values_new <- successful_results %>%
  mutate(EC_values = map(EC_values, ~as.data.frame(.x) %>% 
                           mutate(ECx = c("EC20", "EC10")))) %>%
  unnest(EC_values) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-model_summary, -Lower, -Upper) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, ECx, everything()) %>% 
  rename(se = `Std. Error`) %>% 
  mutate(Estimate = round(Estimate, 6),
         se = round(se, 5)) %>% 
  mutate(Estimate_gl = Estimate * 3.3 / 1,
         se_gl = se * 3.3 / 1,
         Estimate_corr = if_else(Estimate_gl > 3.33, NA, round(Estimate_gl, 6)),
         se_corr = if_else(Estimate_gl > 3.33, NA, round(se_gl, 5)))
ec_values_new

##CEx used-----------------# 

# Step 4: Add these new rows to the original dataframe
df_all_ECx_used <- bind_rows(df_all, new_rows) %>%
  arrange(FN, Dilu) %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "used", state),
         Tps = if_else(is.na(Tps), "30", Tps)) %>%
  filter(state != "used+") %>% 
  filter(FN != "dmso", Tps == "30")
df_all_ECx_used

# Apply the function to each combination of FN and state
results <- df_all_ECx_used %>%
  filter(state == "used") %>%
  group_by(FN, state) %>%
  group_modify(~process_fn_state(.x))
results

# Access specific results
successful_results <- results %>%
  filter(!map_lgl(model_summary, ~is.character(.x[[1]])))
successful_results

ec_values_used <- successful_results %>%
  mutate(EC_values = map(EC_values, ~as.data.frame(.x) %>% 
                           mutate(ECx = c("EC20", "EC10")))) %>%
  unnest(EC_values) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-model_summary, -Lower, -Upper) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, ECx, everything()) %>% 
  rename(se = `Std. Error`) %>% 
  mutate(Estimate = round(Estimate, 6),
         se = round(se, 5)) %>% 
  mutate(Estimate_gl = Estimate * 3.3 / 1,
         se_gl = se * 3.3 / 1,
         Estimate_corr = if_else(Estimate_gl > 3.33, NA, round(Estimate_gl, 6)),
         se_corr = if_else(Estimate_gl > 3.33, NA, round(se_gl, 5)))
ec_values_used

#Final df
ec_values_df <- bind_rows(ec_values_new, ec_values_used) 
ec_values_df

```

```{r Verification CEx}
pbs_d60_new <- df_all_ECx_new %>% filter(state == "new", FN == "pbs_d60", Tps == "30") %>%
```

```{r final df CEx long}

library(kableExtra)
format_number <- function(x, digits) {
  sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f"), round(x, digits))
}

#pivot_wider to get EC50 and EC10 variables in format value ± se
ECx_all_wider_gl <- ec_values_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, ECx, Estimate_gl, se_gl) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = ECx, values_from = c(Estimate_gl, se_gl))  %>%
  rename(EC20 = Estimate_gl_EC20, EC20_se = se_gl_EC20,
         EC10 = Estimate_gl_EC10, EC10_se = se_gl_EC10) %>%
 #in all double variable all value > 10000 become 10000
  mutate(EC20 = if_else(EC20 > 3.33, 100, EC20),
         EC20_se = if_else(EC20_se > 3.33, 100, EC20_se),
         EC10 = if_else(EC10 > 3.33, 100, EC10),
         EC10_se = if_else(EC10_se > 3.33, 100, EC10_se)) %>% 
  #merge EC et se
  mutate(EC20 = paste0(format_number(EC20, 2), " ± ", format_number(EC20_se, 2)),
         EC10 = paste0(format_number(EC10, 2), " ± ", format_number(EC10_se, 2)),
         EC20 = if_else(grepl("100", EC20), ">3.33", EC20),
         EC10 = if_else(grepl("100", EC10), ">3.33", EC10)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, EC20, EC10) %>% 
  rename("EC20±SE (g/L)" = EC20, "EC10±SE (g/L)" = EC10) %>% 
  #Manual modification of the data due to some outliers
  mutate(`EC20±SE (g/L)` = if_else(`EC20±SE (g/L)` == "0.00 ± 0.00", "1.38 ± 0.72", `EC20±SE (g/L)`),
         `EC10±SE (g/L)` = if_else(`EC10±SE (g/L)` == "0.00 ± 0.00", "0.18 ± 0.22", `EC10±SE (g/L)`)) %>%
  #adding manually rows that are not present
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_g33", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33", `EC10±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_g60", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33", `EC10±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_b33", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33", `EC10±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>%
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_b95", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33", `EC10±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  arrange(FN, state)

ECx_all_wider_gl

df_forma_MS <- ECx_all_wider_gl %>% 
  arrange(FN, state) %>%  
  kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE, align = rep('c', ncol(.))) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") 

df_forma_MS

```

```{r final df CEx wider}

library(kableExtra)
format_number <- function(x, digits) {
  sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f"), round(x, digits))
}

#pivot_wider to get EC50 and EC10 variables in format value ± se
ECx_all_wider_gl <- ec_values_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, ECx, Estimate_gl, se_gl) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = ECx, values_from = c(Estimate_gl, se_gl))  %>%
  rename(EC20 = Estimate_gl_EC20, EC20_se = se_gl_EC20,
         EC10 = Estimate_gl_EC10, EC10_se = se_gl_EC10) %>%
 #in all double variable all value > 10000 become 10000
  mutate(EC20 = if_else(EC20 > 3.33, 100, EC20),
         EC20_se = if_else(EC20_se > 3.33, 100, EC20_se),
         EC10 = if_else(EC10 > 3.33, 100, EC10),
         EC10_se = if_else(EC10_se > 3.33, 100, EC10_se)) %>% 
  #merge EC et se
  mutate(EC20 = paste0(format_number(EC20, 2), " ± ", format_number(EC20_se, 2)),
         EC10 = paste0(format_number(EC10, 2), " ± ", format_number(EC10_se, 2)),
         EC20 = if_else(grepl("100", EC20), ">3.33", EC20),
         EC10 = if_else(grepl("100", EC10), ">3.33", EC10)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, EC20) %>% 
  rename("EC20±SE (g/L)" = EC20) %>% 
  #Manual modification of the data due to some outliers
  mutate(`EC20±SE (g/L)` = if_else(`EC20±SE (g/L)` == "0.00 ± 0.00", "1.38 ± 0.72", `EC20±SE (g/L)`)) %>%
  #adding manually rows that are not present
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_g33", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_g60", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_b33", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>%
  bind_rows(., tibble(FN = "pa_b95", state = "used", `EC20±SE (g/L)` = ">3.33")) %>% 
  arrange(FN, state) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = state, values_from = c(`EC20±SE (g/L)`)) %>% 
  mutate(FN = if_else(grepl("pa_g60", FN), "PA \u03B360",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_g33", FN), "PA \u03B333",
                                       if_else(grepl("pbs_d60", FN), "PBS \u03B460",
                                               if_else(grepl("pbs_d35", FN), "PBS \u03B435",
                                                       if_else(grepl("hppe_e425", FN), "HPPE \u03B54.25",
                                                               if_else(grepl("pa_b95", FN), "PA \u03B295",
                      if_else(grepl("pa_b33", FN), "PA \u03B233",
                              if_else(grepl("pa_a35", FN), "PA \u03B135", FN))))))))) %>% 
  rename("Fishing nets" = FN, "New FN EC20" = new, "Used FN EC20" = used)
ECx_all_wider_gl

#export xlsx
# library(openxlsx)
# write.xlsx(ECx_all_wider_gl, "CE20_publi_dmso.xlsx")

ECx_all_wider_gl

df_forma_MS <- ECx_all_wider_gl %>% 
  kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE, align = rep('c', ncol(.))) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") 

df_forma_MS

```


```{r toxicity score}

dmso_tox_sco <- df_avg %>% 
  filter(FN == "dmso") 
dmso_tox_sco

Tox_sco_survival <- df_avg %>% 
  filter(FN != "dmso") %>% 
  mutate(m_effect = abs(mean-dmso_tox_sco$mean), #1.68 is the percentage of dead larvae is the control of the used ones
         score = m_effect/3.3*1) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, m_effect, score) %>% 
  filter(state != "used+")  %>% 
  mutate(score = if_else(FN == "hppe_e425" | FN == "pa_g33" | FN == "pa_g60", 0, score), 
         score = if_else(FN == "pa_b33" & state == "used", 0, score))
Tox_sco_survival

#export as csv
#write.csv(Tox_sco_survival, "/Users/4p_dusacre/ownCloud/PhD/SCIENCES/Manips/Tox/Ethovision/dmso_tox_score/Tox_sco_biolu.csv")

IBR_microtox <- df_all %>% 
  filter(Tps == "30", Dilu == 1 | Conc == 0) %>% 
  # Step 1: Duplicate rows where FN == "dmso"
  slice(rep(1:n(), ifelse(FN == "dmso", 2, 1))) %>%
  # Step 2: Create a new column to alternate "new" and "used" for duplicated rows
  mutate(new_state = case_when(
    FN == "dmso" & is.na(state) ~ rep(c("new", "used"), length.out = n()),
    TRUE ~ state
  )) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Step 3: Replace the original state column
  dplyr::select(-state) %>%
  rename(state = new_state) %>% 
  mutate(ID_IBR = paste(FN, state, sep = "_")) %>% 
  filter(state != "used+") %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, ID_IBR, per_Inhi) 
IBR_microtox

#export as csv
#write.csv(IBR_microtox, "C:/Users/edusacre/ownCloud/PhD/SCIENCES/Manips/Tox/IBR_tables/IBR_microtox.csv")
```


```{r Correlation using time}

df_used_time <- df_avg %>% 
  filter(state == "used") %>% 
  mutate(U_time = if_else(FN == "pa_g60", 624,
                          if_else(FN == "pa_g33", 624,
                                  if_else(FN == "pbs_d60", 624,
                                          if_else(FN == "pbs_d35", 624,
                                                  if_else(FN == "hppe_e425", 8280,
                                                          if_else(FN == "pa_b95", 2700,
                                                                  if_else(FN == "pa_b33", 2700,
                                                                          if_else(FN == "pa_a35", 640, NA))))))))) 

df_used_time

correlation <- cor.test(df_used_time$mean, df_used_time$U_time, method = "spearman")
correlation

# Créer le graphique avec ggplot2
ggplot(df_used_time, aes(x = U_time, y = mean)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue") + # Points en bleu
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) + # Régression linéaire avec intervalle de confiance
  labs(title = "Correlation between per_Inhni and U_time",
       x = "U_time (hours)",
       y = "per_Inhi") +
  theme_bw() 

```

No correlation between the bioluminescence inhibition and the time for which the Fn were used for.

```{r Correlation surface exchanges}

df_S_Ex <- df_avg %>% 
  filter(FN != "dmso", state != "used+", state == "used") %>% 
  mutate(S_Ex = if_else(FN == "pa_g60", 34.1,
                          if_else(FN == "pa_g33", 52.1,
                                  if_else(FN == "pbs_d60", 26.8,
                                          if_else(FN == "pbs_d35", 40.4,
                                                  if_else(FN == "hppe_e425", 11.7,
                                                          if_else(FN == "pa_b95", 95.3,
                                                                  if_else(FN == "pa_b33", 53.0,
                                                                          if_else(FN == "pa_a35", 66.6, NA))))))))) 

df_S_Ex

correlation <- cor.test(df_S_Ex$mean, df_S_Ex$S_Ex, method = "spearman")
correlation

# Créer le graphique avec ggplot2
ggplot(df_S_Ex, aes(x = S_Ex, y = mean)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue") + # Points en bleu
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) + # Régression linéaire avec intervalle de confiance
  labs(title = "Correlation between per_Inhni and U_time",
       x = "U_time (hours)",
       y = "per_Inhi") +
  theme_bw() 

```

No correlation between the bioluminescence inhibition and the FN's surface exchanges.
